title: How to unpledge securities?
text:
You can
sell pledged holdings instantly
without placing an unpledge request.

title: How safe is it to pledge the securities with Zerodha?
text:
Pledging securities
with Zerodha is safe because your securities do not physically leave your demat account. As per
SEBI's new pledging mechanism,
when you pledge securities, they remain in your demat account. Instead, a pledge is registered in favour of the broker.
The broker must establish a dedicated demat account designated as the
TMCM - Client Securities Margin Pledge Account
(TMCM stands for Trading Member Clearing Member). Subsequently, the broker re-pledges these securities to the clearing corporation to obtain margins. This approach prevents brokers from misusing client securities since your securities always remain in your demat account.
To learn more, visit
zerodha.com/z-connect/zerodha/announcements/understanding-the-new-margin-pledge-system.

title: Which stocks and mutual funds can be pledged with Zerodha for collateral margins?
text:
You can find the list of stocks and mutual funds that can be pledged with Zerodha for margins and the applicable haircut percentage by visiting
zerodha.com/approved-securities.
They are divided into cash and non-cash components. However,
some holdings cannot be pledged.
Cash vs non-cash components
Non-cash component securities:
You can use collateral margin from non-cash component securities only up to 50% of the margin requirement for your open positions. The exchange requires the remaining 50% as cash or equivalent collateral. Additional utilisation beyond 50% incurs
delayed payment charges.
Cash component securities:
You can use collateral margin from cash component securities fully towards any margin requirement for your open positions without maintaining separate cash.
Things to keep in mind
The cost of pledging at Zerodha is ₹30 + GST per pledge request, irrespective of the quantity pledged. There is no cost for unpledging.
Treasury Bills (T-Bills) with maturities of 364 and 182 days can now be pledged for collateral margin. T-Bills with maturities of 91 days will soon be available.

title: Will Zerodha provide margin on liquid funds?
text:
Yes, Zerodha provides margins on liquid mutual funds when you
pledge them as collateral.
Cash equivalent securities have a low-risk, low-return profile that includes items that are similar to cash, and liquid funds are cash equivalent securities due to their nature. You can find the list of approved instruments that can be pledged for margins and the applicable haircut percentage by visiting
zerodha.com/approved-securities.
You can view the collateral margin for liquid bees ETFs or liquid mutual funds you have pledged under the
Collateral (liquid funds)
section of Funds on Kite.

title: Can joint bank accounts be linked with a Zerodha account?
text:
Yes, you can link a joint bank account if you are one of the joint holders. You can follow the same procedure as linking a normal single-holder bank account.
If you link a joint bank account to multiple Zerodha accounts, you can only add money using UPI or the payment gateway. Zerodha will reverse the money transferred using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS back to your bank account.

title: Can I link a relative's bank account to my Zerodha account?
text: No, SEBI mandates that you can only link a bank account in your name with your Zerodha account.

title: Can an Overdraft (OD) bank account be linked with Zerodha?
text:
No, you cannot link an Overdraft (OD) bank account with Zerodha. You can link the following bank account types with both individual and corporate accounts:
Savings account
Current account
Cash Credit (CC) account

title: Can I link a current bank account to my Zerodha account?
text:
Yes, you can link a current account to your Zerodha account as both a primary and secondary bank account, provided the names in your Zerodha account, PAN, and bank account match.
The documents required to link a current account are as follows:
Bank type
Proof
Primary
Courier the following to Zerodha:
Account modification form
(PDF).
Cancelled cheque or recent month’s bank statement.
Banker letter
(PDF).
Secondary
Update the bank details on Console
and
create a ticket
with a self-attested copy of the banker's letter
(PDF).
The banker's letter is required to state that the account holder solely runs the business and will make all transactions.
Courier the above-mentioned documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Your bank account will be linked within 48 working hours. Zerodha charges an account modification charge of ₹25 + GST to change your primary bank account.

title: How to add Zerodha as a beneficiary for fund transfers using HDFC netbanking or other banks?
text:
You can only make fund transfers through
bank accounts that are linked to your Zerodha account.
To add money through HDFC netbanking, add the Zerodha HDFC bank account as a payee by following these steps:
Login to the HDFC netbanking portal.
Click on
Add Payee.
Click on
Add a Merchant (eCMS) Payee.
Enter the payee nickname, Zerodha account number ZERNSE and your email ID.
Click on
Continue.
Accept the terms and conditions and click on
Continue.
Enter the OTP.
Click on
Continue.
The beneficiary bank account will be added in 30 minutes. As per your bank's policy, you can transfer ₹50,000 (in full or in part) for the newly added beneficiary during the first 24 hours. After 24 hours, the transfer limit will be your Third-Party Transfer (TPT) limit.
If you do not have an HDFC account, you can still transfer funds to Zerodha by adding the Zerodha eCMS bank account as a beneficiary through your bank's netbanking portal using the eCMS, corporate, or merchant payment options.

title: Why did Zerodha send an email asking to update bank details?
text:
Zerodha sent this email because the bank account linked to your Zerodha account does not match your account category. SEBI regulations require all account details to be valid and up-to-date.
Your bank account may have one of these issues:
Overdraft (OD) account:
You linked an overdraft account instead of a regular savings or current account to your demat account.
Bank name or account number mismatch:
The bank name or account number on your submitted bank statement does not match the details in your
Client Master Report (CMR).
NRE or NRO account linked to a resident account:
You linked a Non-Resident External (NRE) or Non-Resident Ordinary (NRO) bank account to your resident individual Zerodha account, which is not permitted under regulations.
What happens if you do not update your bank details?
You have 30 calendar days from the date of the email to
update your bank details.
If you do not update your bank account within this period:
Zerodha will freeze your account under the invalid bank details category as a precautionary measure.
You will not be able to place any new trades.
Zerodha will block all fund withdrawals from your account.
You will not receive dividends until you link a valid bank account.
Your account will remain frozen until you update your bank account details.

title: What types of bank accounts can I link with Zerodha?
text:
You can link the following types of bank accounts:
Savings account
Current account
Cash Credit account
You cannot link Overdraft (OD) bank accounts. This applies to both individual and corporate Zerodha accounts.

title: Why am I unable to add or change my bank account details?
text:
You might be unable to add or change your bank account details due to the following reasons:
You entered an incorrect IFSC. A common mistake is typing the alphabet O instead of the number 0 or vice versa. The IFSC is an 11-character alpha-numeric code that you need to enter correctly.
CDSL does not recognise your bank branch or IFSC.
If you are still unable to add or change bank details,
create a ticket
and attach a self-attested copy of your bank statement or cheque clearly displaying the IFSC and MICR code. Zerodha will contact CDSL to update the IFSC within 48 working hours, after which you can proceed with adding or changing your bank details.

title: How to change my primary bank account?
text:
Individual account holders and NRO Non-PIS clients can change their primary bank account online. Other account types must follow the offline process.
Online process
Follow these steps to change your primary bank account online:

Console.

Account.

Modify bank account
icon.
Enter the

and then on

Aadhaar number
and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the
OTP
received on the mobile number and click on
Verify OTP.
Zerodha initiates a test transfer to your new primary bank account for verification. If this verification fails, Zerodha will send you an email explaining the issue, and you must follow the offline process.
Joint bank accounts
You can add a joint bank account as your primary bank account if the primary holder of the bank and demat account is the same. If they are not the same, courier the account modification form
(PDF)
and bank proof to Zerodha.
For minor accounts, you can change the primary bank account online by following the above steps. However, the guardian must e-sign using their Aadhaar, and the bank details must be for the minor.
Offline process

and bank proof

(PDF)

to Zerodha. Alternatively, if your mobile number is linked to Aadhaar, you can
esign the forms

creating a ticket.

Ensure that the signature on the documents matches the

Courier your documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre

Zerodha will link your new primary bank account within 72 working hours and send you a confirmation email. A charge of ₹25 + GST applies to change your primary bank account. You can link a maximum of three bank accounts to your trading account: one primary bank account and two secondary bank accounts.
Note
1
Bankproof can be any one of the following:
Personalised cancelled cheque with the name printed on the cheque leaf.
Self-attested bank statement with the IFSC and MICR clearly visible.
Self-attested bank passbook.

title: What is a primary bank account with regards to my Zerodha account?
text:
A primary bank account is the main bank account linked to your Zerodha account. The bank proof you submit while opening your trading and demat account is linked as your primary bank account. You can use your primary bank account for both depositing and withdrawing funds to and from your trading account. You can also
change your primary bank account through Console.
You can add up to 3 bank accounts to your trading account, but only 1
will be treated as the primary, and the other 2 as
secondary.
Did you know?
PayTM payments bank cannot be linked to a Zerodha account.

title: Can I link multiple bank accounts to my Zerodha account?
text:
Yes, you can link multiple bank accounts to your Zerodha account: one primary bank account and two
secondary bank accounts.

title: How to convert a secondary bank account into a primary bank account at Zerodha?
text:
You can convert your secondary bank account into a primary bank account online if your Aadhaar is linked to a mobile number. Follow these steps on Console:
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Bank.
Click on
Set as primary
and then on
Proceed to e-sign.
Accept the Terms and Conditions and click on
Proceed.
Click on
Sign now.
Provide authorisation, enter the Aadhaar number and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP received and click on Verify OTP.
Zerodha processes your request within 72 working hours and sends you a confirmation email. A charge of ₹25 + 18% GST will apply, which Zerodha will debit from your account. When Zerodha converts your secondary bank account to the primary account, your current primary bank account will automatically become the secondary account.
Did you know?
While the interchange is being processed, if the customer places a withdrawal request, the funds get credited to the old primary bank account.
You can also
change your primary bank through Console.

title: How to add a secondary bank account to a Zerodha account?
text:
You can add a secondary bank account by following these steps:

user ID.

Mobile

Web

Console.

Account.

Bank.
Click on
Add bank account.
Enter the account number and IFSC and accept the undertaking.
Select to receive the OTP via e-mail or mobile and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on
Verify.
If a joint bank account is being linked and the Zerodha account holder is not the first holder in the bank account, please create a ticket with any of the following bank proofs:
A self-attested scanned copy of a personalised cancelled cheque with the account holder's name printed on the cheque.
A self-attested copy of the bank statement with the name, IFSC and MICR numbers clearly visible.
A self-attested copy of the bank passbook with the name, IFSC, and MICR numbers clearly visible.
The signature on the bank proof must match the
signature provided while opening an account.
The request is processed within 48 working hours, and a confirmation email is sent. Adding or modifying a secondary bank account is free.
Things to keep in mind
The
secondary bank account can be converted to the primary bank account.
NRE-PIS demat account holders cannot add a secondary bank account.

title: How to add a secondary bank account to a Zerodha account?
text:
You can add a secondary bank account by following these steps:
Kite app
Tap on the
user ID.
Tap on
Profile.
Tap on
Manage account.
Tap on ＞ and then on
Bank.
Tap on
Add bank account.
Enter the bank details and accept the terms and conditions.
Tap on
E-mail
or
Mobile
to receive the OTP and tap on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP.
Tap on
Verify.
Web
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Bank.
Click on
Add bank account.
Enter the account number and IFSC and accept the undertaking.
Select to receive the OTP via e-mail or mobile and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on
Verify.
If a joint bank account is being linked and the Zerodha account holder is not the first holder in the bank account, please create a ticket with any of the following bank proofs:
A self-attested scanned copy of a personalised cancelled cheque with the account holder's name printed on the cheque.
A self-attested copy of the bank statement with the name, IFSC and MICR numbers clearly visible.
A self-attested copy of the bank passbook with the name, IFSC, and MICR numbers clearly visible.
The signature on the bank proof must match the
signature provided while opening an account.
The request is processed within 48 working hours, and a confirmation email is sent. Adding or modifying a secondary bank account is free.
Things to keep in mind
The
secondary bank account can be converted to the primary bank account.
NRE-PIS demat account holders cannot add a secondary bank account.

title: How to change the secondary bank account?
text:
You can change the secondary bank account by following these steps:
Log in to
Console.
Click on the
Account.
Click on Bank.
Click on
Modify bank account
icon.
Enter the
new account number and IFSC,
and accept the terms and conditions.
Select to receive the OTP via e-mail or mobile and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the
OTP
and click on
Verify.
A test transfer is initiated to the new secondary bank account for verification. If this verification fails, or if a joint bank account is being linked and the Zerodha account holder is not the first holder,
create a ticket
with any one of the following bank proofs:
A self-attested scanned copy of a personalised cancelled cheque with the name printed on the cheque leaf.
A self-attested copy of the bank statement with the name, IFSC and MICR numbers clearly visible.
A self-attested copy of the bank passbook with the name, IFSC and MICR numbers clearly visible.
The request is processed within 48 working hours, and a confirmation email is sent. There are no charges to change the secondary bank account.
Individual and non-individual Zerodha account holders can follow the above process to change their secondary bank account.

title: How do I remove a secondary bank account linked to my Zerodha account?
text:
You can follow these steps on
Console
to remove a secondary bank account:
Click on
Account.
Click on
Bank.
Click on the bin icon.
Select to receive the OTP via e-mail or mobile and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on
Verify.
Zerodha removes your secondary bank account instantly and sends you a confirmation email.
You can also
convert your secondary bank account into a primary bank account.

title: What is a secondary bank account?
text:
A secondary bank account is an additional bank account linked to your Zerodha account. You can link up to three bank accounts in total, one primary and two secondary. Both primary and secondary bank accounts can be used to add or withdraw funds. However, withdrawals to a secondary bank account can only be processed if it is
penny-drop verified.
Secondary bank accounts can be
added
or
removed
through Console.

title: Why is the money added through netbanking on Kite not reflecting in the Zerodha account?
text:
When you transfer funds via netbanking on Kite and they don't appear instantly in your Zerodha account, this means your transaction is either pending or has failed at your bank's end. As a result, Zerodha does not receive confirmation of a successful transfer from your bank. Your bank will update the transfer status only after completing its reconciliation process, which takes place on the next bank working day.
You will see one of these outcomes by 1 PM on the next bank working day:
Your funds will be credited to your Zerodha account, or
Your bank will refund the amount to your bank account
If neither occurs,
create a ticket.

title: How much time does it take for the funds to get transferred to the Zerodha account?
text:
The time taken for funds to reach your Zerodha account depends on the transfer method you use. UPI and payment gateway transfers are instant, while other methods take a few minutes to several working days.
Funds transferred to the Zerodha account between 12 AM and 7:30 AM will reflect only after 7:30 AM.
Transfer methods and timelines
Method
Time taken
Charges
NEFT/RTGS
Usually within 2 hours
Bank charges may apply.
IMPS
Usually within 10 minutes
Bank charges may apply.
UPI
Instant
Free
Instant Payment Gateway
Instant
₹9 + 18%GST
Cheque
3-5 working days
Free
Create a ticket
if the funds are not updated within the above timelines.
Things to keep in mind
You can only transfer funds from bank accounts linked to your Zerodha account. You can have multiple bank accounts linked to your account. To view your linked bank accounts, visit
console.zerodha.com/account/bank.
A maximum of ₹1 crore can be added through internet banking using the payment gateway and ₹5 lakh using UPI in a single transaction. To transfer more than ₹1 crore using internet banking, make multiple transfers. However, this will differ based on the bank and the limit prescribed by them.
The minimum amount that can be transferred using internet banking is ₹50, and UPI is ₹1.

title: Why are the funds added today not shown in the funds statement on Console?
text:
When you add funds to your Zerodha account during trading hours, they show up under
Payin
on Kite. At the end of the day, they will start reflecting under the funds statement on Console.

title: Why are funds transferred using UPI not reflecting in the Zerodha account?
text:
If your UPI fund transfer has failed and your bank has debited the funds from your bank account, your bank will reverse the funds. Please check with your bank for further clarification.
If you transfer funds from a UPI ID linked to a bank account not registered with Zerodha, your fund transfer will fail, and the amount will not be credited. Banks can take up to 72 working hours to refund the amount.

title: Why are the funds added through IMPS, NEFT or RTGS not reflected in the Zerodha account?
text:
Transferring funds to your Zerodha account with NEFT or RTGS usually takes 2 hours, while IMPS transactions usually take 10 minutes.
If your funds do not reflect within these timelines, it could be due to the following:
You transferred the funds from an unmapped bank account. As per the SEBI mandate,
funds can be transferred only from bank accounts linked to the Zerodha account.
Zerodha has not received the bank details from the remitting bank, even though you transferred the funds from a mapped bank account. In such cases,
create a ticket.
You can also use
UPI to transfer funds
instantly at no cost.

title: How to add money to the Zerodha account using UPI?
text:
You can add money to your Zerodha account using UPI by following these steps:
Adding funds through Kite app
Tap on the user ID.
Tap on
Funds.
Tap on
Add Funds,
and enter the amount.
Select the UPI application and complete the payment.
Adding funds through Kite web
Under the
Funds
tab, click on
Add funds.
Enter the amount and select UPI.
Enter the UPI ID linked to the bank account registered with Zerodha.
A confirmation page is displayed on the Zerodha payment page after a successful transfer.
You will see the amount instantly under
Pay-in
in the
Funds
section.
If you have installed your UPI app on another device, you need to validate the collect request on that device.
Things to keep in mind
You can make up to 35 transfers per day.
If you transfer money between 12 AM and 7:30 AM, it will not reflect until after 7:30 AM.
You can only add money from your primary or
secondary bank account
linked with Zerodha.
To change your default UPI app, you must clear the default settings on your current UPI app. To change your UPI ID on Android devices, tap on
Netbanking
, then tap on
UPI
and enter your UPI ID.
You can transfer money from your registered bank accounts using the Paytm app.However, the
Paytm Payments Bank cannot be linked to transfer funds.
The UPI limit for capital market investments is ₹5 lakh per transaction.
All UPI transfers must be initiated from Kite. Zerodha does not accept digital wallet transfers or transfers directly from UPI apps. When adding funds to Kite, you will only receive requests from these official UPI IDs:
zerodhabroking.brk@validhdfc
zerodhabroking.brk@validicici
zerodhabroking.brk@validaxis

title: Why is money transferred via UPI not added to my Zerodha account?
text:
If you consistently face issues with one UPI app, try using a different UPI app.
Your money may not be added to your Zerodha account even after a successful UPI transfer. This happens when you transfer money directly to Zerodha's UPI ID instead of using the
Add Funds
option on Kite, or when your UPI transaction fails due to technical issues.
Why your UPI transfer may fail
Your UPI transaction fails when adding money to your Zerodha account for these reasons:
You transferred money directly to Zerodha's VPA or UPI ID from your UPI app instead of using the
Add Funds
option on Kite or Coin.
Your UPI ID is linked to an unregistered bank account. You can only transfer money from bank accounts registered with Zerodha. UPI transactions from unregistered bank accounts automatically fail and are reversed within 72 working hours. If your funds are not reversed, please contact your bank
You did not accept the UPI request within 5 minutes.
You exceeded the daily transaction limit. You can make only 35 UPI transactions per day.
You’ve exceeded your bank’s UPI transfer limit. For capital market investments, UPI transfers are capped at ₹5 lakh per transaction, and your bank may apply additional restrictions per transaction.
You entered an incorrect UPI ID (VPA).
What to do if your transfer fails
If your UPI transfer fails, the amount will be automatically reversed to your bank account within 72 working hours. Please contact your bank if the reversal takes longer.

title: How to add money using netbanking on Kite?

You can add money to your Zerodha account using Netbanking for instant credit. However, there are charges of ₹9 + 18% GST per transfer, and the minimum amount you can add is ₹50.
Alternative:

for free.

After authorising the payment, a window will pop up shortly with the details and status of the transaction.

Things to keep in mind

IMPS, NEFT or RTGS.
Funds transferred between 12:00 AM and 7:30 AM will not reflect until after 7:30 AM.
Zerodha accepts fund transfers only from your linked bank accounts. View your linked bank accounts by visiting
console.zerodha.com/account/bank.
You can link
more than one bank account to Zerodha.

title: How to add money to Zerodha using a cheque?
text:
You can add money to your Zerodha account using a cheque for free. Your account will be updated once the cheque clears, which can take up to 5 working days.
Steps to deposit a cheque
Write
Zerodha Broking Ltd
as the payee on your cheque.
Write your Kite user ID on the back of the cheque.
Courier the cheque to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor, Kalyani Vista
3rd Main Road, JP Nagar 4th Phase
Bengaluru, 560076
If you deposit at an HDFC branch
If you deposit the cheque directly at any HDFC branch,
create a ticket
and attach a scanned copy of the cheque or your debited bank statement that includes your bank account number. You need to provide this to verify that you transferred the amount from your bank account linked with Zerodha, since Zerodha does not receive the sender's bank account details.
If the bank asks you to mention Zerodha's account number at the back of the cheque, you can write:
57500000302010.
Things to keep in mind
You will be charged up to ₹350 + 18% GST if your cheque bounces
Zerodha will only accept money transferred from your linked bank accounts. You can link more than one bank account with Zerodha. To see your linked bank accounts, visit
console.zerodha.com/account/bank
You can transfer money instantly to your Zerodha account using
UPI,
payment gateway,
and
IMPS/NEFT/RTGS.

title: How to transfer money to your Zerodha account using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS?
text:
You can transfer money to your Zerodha account by adding Zerodha as a beneficiary in your internet banking portal and transferring funds using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS. All transfers from your linked bank accounts are free at Zerodha, though your bank may charge fees.
Bank account details for fund transfers
Use these details when adding Zerodha as a beneficiary:

Charges

NEFT/RTGS
Within 2 hours
Free at Zerodha, bank charges may apply
You can add
Zerodha as a beneficiary for fund transfers using linked bank accounts.
If you cannot add the beneficiary or do not see the funds in your Zerodha account within the specified timelines,
create a ticket
for assistance.
Things to keep in mind
You can only transfer funds from bank accounts linked to your Zerodha account. You can have multiple bank accounts linked to your account. To view your linked bank accounts, visit
console.zerodha.com/account/bank
Only fund transfers for more than ₹1 will be updated on the terminal
If you have a joint bank account linked to multiple Zerodha accounts, you can only add money using UPI or the payment gateway. Money transferred using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS will be reversed back to your bank account
Do not transfer funds to Zerodha's Yes Bank payout account ending in ******0071, as these transactions must be manually reversed to your account. Only use the bank account details mentioned above. If you have already transferred funds to the Yes Bank account, send a soft copy of your bank statement showing the debit transaction by
creating a ticket
If you have an NRI PIS account, do not transfer funds using IMPS/NEFT/RTGS. To learn more, see
What is the trading process for NRI accounts?
If you transfer funds to your trading account between 12 AM and 7:30 AM, it won't reflect on the trading terminal until after 7:30 AM.

title: What are the different ways to transfer money to your Zerodha account?
text:
You can transfer funds to your Zerodha account in three different ways:
UPI:
Transfer funds instantly from your registered bank account using
UPI
for free. You must initiate UPI transfers from Zerodha's payment gateway for the funds to be updated in your Zerodha account instantly.
Netbanking:
You can use the
Netbanking facility to transfer funds to your Zerodha account.
However, there are charges of ₹9 + 18% GST.
IMPS/NEFT/RTGS/Cheque:
You can
transfer funds using IMPS/NEFT/RTGS
for free at Zerodha. However, your bank may apply charges. You can also
transfer funds using a cheque
for free.
Things to keep in mind
Zerodha accepts UPI transfers made only through the
Add Funds
interface on Kite. Digital wallet transfers or transfers initiated directly from a UPI app will not be accepted.
If you receive an email or phone call claiming to be from Zerodha soliciting fund transfers to an account number that is not mentioned on the
Funds page
or if you notice any spoofed (duplicate/unofficial) Zerodha website,
create a ticket
immediately with those details. Never provide sensitive account information like support code, password, client ID or personal details in response to an email.
SEBI regulations do not allow Zerodha or any other broker to accept deposits via cash/Demand Draft (DD).

title: How to add money on Kite app if your UPI app is on a different device?
text:
You can add money to your Zerodha account through the Kite app even if your UPI app is installed on a different device. You will need to validate the transfer on the device where your UPI app is installed.
Steps to add money
Tap on
Add funds.
Enter the amount you want to transfer and tap on
Netbanking.
Tap on
UPI
and enter your UPI ID.
Validate the transfer on your UPI app on the other device.
Things to keep in mind
You can make up to 35 UPI transfers per day, with each transfer up to ₹5 lakh. Your bank may impose additional restrictions on the amount per transaction.
If you transfer funds between 12 AM and 7:30 AM, they will not reflect in your Zerodha account until after 7:30 AM.
You can only transfer funds from a UPI ID linked to your bank account registered with Zerodha.

title: Can I transfer funds using a digital wallet?
text: No, you cannot transfer funds using a digital wallet. Regulations require you to transfer funds only from your verified bank accounts. However, you can transfer funds using UPI.

title: Can I transfer funds to Zerodha account using debit or credit card?
text:
No, you cannot transfer funds to your Zerodha account using a debit or credit card. You also cannot use credit cards for any saving and investing products, such as fixed deposits, investments in stock markets etc. This applies to all brokers.
Regulations mandate that you transfer funds through the trading app and other approved payment methods. You can transfer money to your Zerodha account using
UPI,
payment gateway,
IMPS/NEFT/RTGS,
or through
cheque.

title: Can I transfer funds to my Zerodha account using cash or demand draft?
text:
No, you cannot transfer funds using cash or demand draft. Regulations mandate that you transfer funds through the trading app and
other approved payment methods,
which applies to all brokers.

title: Can funds be transferred using IMPS, NEFT, RTGS or cheque from bank accounts not linked to the Zerodha account?
text:
As per the SEBI mandate, funds can be transferred only from bank accounts linked to the Zerodha account. Any funds transferred through IMPS, NEFT, RTGS, or cheque from an unlinked bank account are refunded to the source bank account within 24-48 working hours. If the reversal does not occur within this timeframe, please
create a ticket
and attach a bank statement showing the transaction details.
Multiple bank accounts can be linked to a Zerodha account, i.e., one primary bank account and two secondary bank accounts.
Did you know?
An Overdraft (OD) bank account cannot be linked with Zerodha.

title: Can funds in the equity segment be used to trade in commodities?
text:
Yes, clients can use funds from the equity segment to trade in the commodities segment through the
single ledger facility.
To use this facility, clients must activate the commodity segment from Console.

title: Why have I received a UPI collect request from Zerodha, which I did not raise?
text:
You might receive a wrong UPI collect request from Zerodha in the following scenarios:
Another Zerodha client entered your UPI ID by mistake
This happens when another client mistakenly enters your UPI ID while adding funds to their Kite account.
Example scenario:
Two Zerodha account holders: Ashwin K (UPI ID: ashwin.k@okaxis) and Ashwin L (UPI ID: ashwini.l@okaxis)
If Ashwin L enters ashwin.k@okaxis instead of their own UPI ID, you (Ashwin K) will receive the wrong request
What you should do:
Decline the transaction on your UPI app if you did not initiate the transfer. Even if you approve the transaction by mistake, the fund transfer will fail because your bank account is not mapped to their Zerodha account. Zerodha only accepts fund transfers from linked bank accounts.
You can check your linked bank accounts by visiting
console.zerodha.com/account/bank.
Someone has access to your Zerodha account
If someone has unauthorised access to your account, they might place requests for you to add funds. This could happen if:
You shared your Zerodha login details with someone
You fell victim to a phishing attack where fraudsters tricked you into adding funds to your account
What you should do:
Immediately
reset your Kite login credentials.
Learn more about phishing attacks by visiting
zerodha.com/z-connect/phishing-scam.
The fraudster's plan is typically to execute unauthorised transactions with the transferred funds. Learn about common stock market scams by visiting
zerodha.com/z-connect/stock-market-scams.
A fraudster registered Zerodha as their name in a UPI app
Scammers sometimes register "Zerodha" as their display name in UPI apps to trick users into transferring money.
Zerodha's official UPI IDs
When adding funds to Kite, you will only receive requests from these official UPI IDs:
zerodhabroking@hdfcbank
zerodha.broking@icici
zerodhabroking@axisbank
zerodhabroking@yesbank
zerodhabroking.brk@validhdfc
zerodhabroking.brk@validicici
zerodhabroking.brk@validaxis
zerodhabrokingsignup@hdfcbank (when you open your account with Zerodha)
What you should do:
If you receive requests from any other UPI ID claiming to be Zerodha, email us immediately at
[email protected]
or
create a ticket.

title: Can funds in the equity segment be used to trade in commodities?
text:
Yes, clients can use funds from the equity segment to trade in the commodities segment through the
single ledger facility.
To use this facility, clients must activate the commodity segment from Console.

title: Why are funds added through IMPS, NEFT or RTGS getting refunded to the source bank account?
text:
Funds transferred via IMPS, NEFT or RTGS may be refunded to the source bank account for several reasons:
Unlinked bank account:
If a transfer is made from a bank account not linked to the trading account, the funds are automatically refunded. To learn more, see
Can funds be transferred using IMPS, NEFT, RTGS or cheque from bank accounts not linked to the Zerodha account?
Incorrect details:
If the bank account number or IFSC code is incorrect or outdated in the Zerodha account, the funds will be refunded. This may happen if the bank has changed its branch details. To learn more, see
What to do if the bank has changed the branch IFSC, MICR or account number?
Joint bank account:
If funds are transferred via IMPS, NEFT or RTGS from a joint bank account linked to multiple Zerodha accounts, they will be refunded. To learn more, see
Can joint bank accounts be linked with a Zerodha account?
Inactive account:
If the trading account is inactive or has not yet been activated after opening, the funds may be refunded. To learn more see,
How long does it take to open an account, and where can the account opening status be tracked?
Bank settlement account:
If the funds are transferred from a bank's settlement account number, they will not be added to the Zerodha account and will be refunded. In such cases, use other
available fund transfer methods.

title: What are the limits on fund transfers and fund withdrawals in Zerodha?
text:
You can add funds using net banking, UPI, NEFT, RTGS, IMPS, and cheque. You can withdraw any amount up to your available withdrawable balance.
Fund transfer limits
The fund transfer limits for various modes of payment per day are as follows:
Net banking on Kite:
You can make up to 25 net banking transfers per day. Zerodha does not impose any limits on transfer amounts. However, your bank may have restrictions on transfer amounts.
UPI:
You can make up to 35 UPI transfers per day, with each transfer up to ₹5 lakh. Your bank may impose additional restrictions on the amount per transaction.
NEFT and RTGS:
Zerodha does not impose any limits on NEFT and RTGS transfers. However, your bank may have restrictions on transfer amounts.
IMPS:
Most banks allow IMPS transfers up to ₹2 lakh per transaction. Zerodha does not impose any limits, but your bank may have restrictions on the amount per transaction.
Withdrawal limits
You can withdraw any amount (minimum ₹1) subject to the availability of
withdrawable balance
in your trading account. For withdrawals up to ₹5 crores, you can place a request directly from Console. However, if you wish to withdraw more than ₹5 crores, please
create a ticket.
For
instant withdrawals,
the withdrawal requests must be for a minimum of ₹100 and not exceed ₹2,00,000 per day.

title: Why is money transferred via UPI not added to my Zerodha account?
text:
If you consistently face issues with one UPI app, try using a different UPI app.
Your money may not be added to your Zerodha account even after a successful UPI transfer. This happens when you transfer money directly to Zerodha's UPI ID instead of using the
Add Funds
option on Kite, or when your UPI transaction fails due to technical issues.
Why your UPI transfer may fail
Your UPI transaction fails when adding money to your Zerodha account for these reasons:
You transferred money directly to Zerodha's VPA or UPI ID from your UPI app instead of using the
Add Funds
option on Kite or Coin.
Your UPI ID is linked to an unregistered bank account. You can only transfer money from bank accounts registered with Zerodha. UPI transactions from unregistered bank accounts automatically fail and are reversed within 72 working hours. If your funds are not reversed, please contact your bank
You did not accept the UPI request within 5 minutes.
You exceeded the daily transaction limit. You can make only 35 UPI transactions per day.
You’ve exceeded your bank’s UPI transfer limit. For capital market investments, UPI transfers are capped at ₹5 lakh per transaction, and your bank may apply additional restrictions per transaction.
You entered an incorrect UPI ID (VPA).
What to do if your transfer fails
If your UPI transfer fails, the amount will be automatically reversed to your bank account within 72 working hours. Please contact your bank if the reversal takes longer.

title: What are the names of the payment gateway vendors in Zerodha?
text:
Zerodha uses NTT DATA payment services (ATOM) and Pine labs payment gateway (Billdesk) as its payment gateway vendors. When funds are transferred to Zerodha accounts through net banking, clients are redirected to them.
However, if funds are transferred through an IDFC 3-in-1 account, ICICI, SBI, HDFC, Kotak or Axis bank, clients will not be redirected to NTT or Pine labs as Zerodha has a direct payment gateway with these banks. To learn how to transfer funds using net banking, see
How to add funds using netbanking on Kite?

title: What to do if the bank has changed the branch IFSC, MICR or account number?
text:
Sometimes, banks undergo internal restructuring, get merged or acquired, due to which the IFSC, MICR and account numbers can change.
In such cases, the account details must be updated with Zerodha. The details can be updated by updating the bank account online. If the primary bank account details are changed, see
How to change the primary bank account?
to update the primary bank account details. If the secondary bank account details are changed, remove the existing secondary bank account and relink the bank account with the updated details. See
How to remove a secondary bank account linked to a Zerodha account?
and
How to add a secondary bank account to a Zerodha account?
to learn more.
The online modification is possible only if the mobile number is linked to an Aadhaar. If the Aadhaar is unlinked or if a non-individual account (HUF, Corporate, or Partnership) is held, follow the offline process explained below.
Download the account modification form
(PDF),
and send it to Zerodha's head office along with the following bank account proof:
Personalised cancelled cheque with the account holder's name printed on the cheque leaf.
Self-attested bank statement with IFSC and MICR clearly visible.
Bank passbook.
Courier the forms to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Did you know?
The bank account must be in the name of the Zerodha account holder. A charge of ₹25 + 18% GST will be levied for primary bank account modification.

title: Why have I received a UPI collect request from Zerodha, which I did not raise?
text:
You might receive a wrong UPI collect request from Zerodha in the following scenarios:
Another Zerodha client entered your UPI ID by mistake
This happens when another client mistakenly enters your UPI ID while adding funds to their Kite account.
Example scenario:
Two Zerodha account holders: Ashwin K (UPI ID: ashwin.k@okaxis) and Ashwin L (UPI ID: ashwini.l@okaxis)
If Ashwin L enters ashwin.k@okaxis instead of their own UPI ID, you (Ashwin K) will receive the wrong request
What you should do:
Decline the transaction on your UPI app if you did not initiate the transfer. Even if you approve the transaction by mistake, the fund transfer will fail because your bank account is not mapped to their Zerodha account. Zerodha only accepts fund transfers from linked bank accounts.
You can check your linked bank accounts by visiting
console.zerodha.com/account/bank.
Someone has access to your Zerodha account
If someone has unauthorised access to your account, they might place requests for you to add funds. This could happen if:
You shared your Zerodha login details with someone
You fell victim to a phishing attack where fraudsters tricked you into adding funds to your account
What you should do:
Immediately
reset your Kite login credentials.
Learn more about phishing attacks by visiting
zerodha.com/z-connect/phishing-scam.
The fraudster's plan is typically to execute unauthorised transactions with the transferred funds. Learn about common stock market scams by visiting
zerodha.com/z-connect/stock-market-scams.
A fraudster registered Zerodha as their name in a UPI app
Scammers sometimes register "Zerodha" as their display name in UPI apps to trick users into transferring money.
Zerodha's official UPI IDs
When adding funds to Kite, you will only receive requests from these official UPI IDs:
zerodhabroking@hdfcbank
zerodha.broking@icici
zerodhabroking@axisbank
zerodhabroking@yesbank
zerodhabroking.brk@validhdfc
zerodhabroking.brk@validicici
zerodhabroking.brk@validaxis
zerodhabrokingsignup@hdfcbank (when you open your account with Zerodha)
What you should do:
If you receive requests from any other UPI ID claiming to be Zerodha, email us immediately at
[email protected]
or
create a ticket.

title: Why are the funds transferred through IMPS not reflecting in the Zerodha account?
text:
Funds transferred through IMPS usually take up to 10 minutes to reflect in the Zerodha account after transferring during market hours. Fund transfers only from linked bank accounts are accepted. If funds are transferred from an unlinked bank account, see
Can funds be transferred using IMPS, NEFT, RTGS or cheque from bank accounts not linked to the Zerodha account?
to learn how to reverse such transfers.

title: Why did I receive a message saying my withdrawal request was processed when I never made one?
text:
You receive email and SMS notifications when Zerodha transfers funds back to your primary bank account due to
quarterly settlements,
even though you didn't request a withdrawal. This is an automatic process that occurs as part of regulatory requirements.

title: How is withdrawable balance calculated after pledging?
text:
When you pledge securities in your demat account to trade F&O, your withdrawable balance changes based on the collateral margin blocked for your F&O positions and the cash in your account.
At least 50% of the margins blocked for F&O positions have to come in the form of cash or cash equivalent collateral. The other 50% of the margin requirement can come in the form of other non-cash equivalent collateral margins.
Example scenario
You initiate an F&O position requiring ₹1,00,000 margin. Before opening this position, you have:
Cash: ₹70,000
Collateral margin: ₹80,000 from pledged securities (after applying required haircut)
Cash equivalent: ₹20,000 from pledged liquidbees
Here's how the system allocates your ₹1,00,000 F&O margin:
Component
Available for use (INR)
Utilised (INR)
Balance (INR)
Collateral Margin
80,000
50,000
30,000
Cash Equivalent
20,000
20,000
0.00
Cash
70,000
30,000
40,000
Total Margin collected
1,00,000
This calculation leaves you with a withdrawable balance of ₹40,000.
Withdrawable balance formula
The system calculates your withdrawable balance using this formula:
Withdrawable balance = (Available cash balance - Proceeds from stocks sold today - F&O profits today - Funds added today)
Where:
Unsettled credits = Today's credits in equity segment + Today's credits in the derivative segment
Collateral utilised = Total collateral used towards margin requirements from cash and non-cash equivalents
If you don't have enough collateral margins to cover 50% of the blocked margin, the system charges the difference from your available cash balance, which further reduces your withdrawable balance.
Things to keep in mind
Collateral margin from pledged stocks or liquid mutual funds for F&O trading does not count as part of your withdrawable balance.
Funds you add during the day become available for trading immediately. However, you can only withdraw these funds after one day due to end-of-day balance reconciliation requirements.
You can track securities that form part of cash and non-cash components on the approved list by visiting
zerodha.com/approved-securities.

title: Can I withdraw money to my secondary bank account?
text:
Yes, you can withdraw money to any bank account linked with Zerodha by
selecting the preferred bank
when withdrawing funds. However, the bank account must be
penny drop verified
to process the withdrawal.

title: Why are the sales proceeds not withdrawable on the same day?
text:
If you have closed your positions today, you cannot withdraw the funds immediately since exchanges in India follow a
T+1 rolling settlement cycle.
Same-day withdrawal conditions
If you have not traded during the day, have no open positions, have not placed an instant withdrawal request and have sufficient balance on the day of withdrawal, Zerodha will process your withdrawal request on the same day. If you place your request before 5 PM, your money will be credited to your bank account on the same day. However, if you place your request after 5 PM, your money will be credited on the next day.
Example scenario
You sell shares on Monday.
The funds from your sale are credited the next day, i.e. Tuesday.
If you execute no additional trades and submit a fund withdrawal request, Zerodha will credit the funds by the end of the same day, i.e. Tuesday.

title: How to withdraw money from the Zerodha account?
text:
You can place a withdrawal request by following these steps:
Kite app
Tap on user ID.
Tap on
Funds.
Tap on
Withdraw.
Enter the amount to be withdrawn, tap on
Continue.
If multiple bank accounts are linked,
select your preferred bank account.
Tap on
Confirm.
Kite web
Log in to
Kite.
Click on
Funds.
Click on
Withdraw.
Enter the amount to be withdrawn and click on
Continue
If multiple bank accounts are linked,
select your preferred bank account.
Click on
Confirm.
Console
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Funds.
Click on
Withdrawal.
Enter the amount to be withdrawn and click on
Continue
If multiple bank accounts are linked,
select your preferred bank account.
Click on
Confirm.
Things to keep in mind
You can only withdraw the amount shown as your
withdrawable balance.
You cannot withdraw money you added to your account on the same day.
Your withdrawal may be rejected, or you may receive a lower amount if your balance reduces after the trade settlement process at the end of the day.
If you have not traded during the day, have no open positions, have not placed an instant withdrawal request and have a sufficient balance on the withdrawal day, your withdrawal request will be processed on the same day. For same-day withdrawals, money will be credited to your bank account on the same day if you place the request before 5 PM. If you place the request after 5 PM, you will receive the money the next day.
If you have placed CNC sell orders, your regular withdrawal request will only be processed at 10:00 PM.
You can also withdraw money instantly from your Zerodha account using the
instant withdrawal feature.
For detailed information about withdrawal processing times, visit
How much time does it take to process a withdrawal request?

title: How to withdraw money instantly in Zerodha, and how does it work?
text:
If you encounter the
Instant Payouts window is closed
error before 9 AM, or if this error persists after 9 AM, close the Console withdrawal page and retry after 15 minutes.
Zerodha's instant withdrawal feature allows you to withdraw money from your Zerodha account to your linked bank account immediately, with no charges.
Requirements for instant withdrawal
To use instant withdrawal, you must meet these conditions:
Submit your request only once per day between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM on all days, including weekends
Withdraw a minimum of ₹100 and maximum of ₹2,00,000 per day
No pending or executed orders other than
Longterm (CNC)
sell or
MTF
sell orders, and no open positions other than overnight positions
How to place an instant withdrawal request
Tap on your user ID.
Tap on
Funds.
Tap on
Instant.
Enter the amount. (minimum ₹100, maximum ₹2,00,000).
Tap on
Continue
and then on
Confirm.
Things to keep in mind
You cannot use instant withdrawal for money you added during the same day
Credit from the previous day's sale of holdings will not be included in your withdrawable balance on settlement, and trading holidays, and weekends. For example, credit from holdings sold on 17/05/2024 (Friday) will be available for withdrawal only on 21/05/2024 (Tuesday), since 20/05/2024 (Monday) is a settlement holiday
If your instant withdrawal fails, you must place a regular withdrawal request, as instant withdrawals are only allowed once per day
Your withdrawal amount must be a whole number without any decimal value
Your withdrawable balance on Console will not update immediately after you withdraw money using instant withdrawal
If you have linked an
Orbis account
to Zerodha, you cannot use the instant withdrawal feature.
You can use instant withdrawal even if you have open
Good Till Triggered (GTT)
orders.

title: How to place a fund withdrawal request for Orbis account?
text:
If you have an Orbis account linked to Zerodha, you cannot place fund withdrawal requests on Console. To place a fund withdrawal request for your Orbis account, fill out this form
(DOC),
and the funds will be credited to your primary bank account.
Processing timeline
If you submit your withdrawal request before 6 PM, you will receive the funds on the next working day
If you submit your withdrawal request after 6 PM or during the weekend, you will receive the funds after two working days
Example scenario
You place a withdrawal request on Wednesday at 7:00 PM.
Zerodha will process your request on Thursday.
The funds are credited on Friday to your primary bank account linked to Zerodha.
Things to keep in mind
If you hold an Orbis account, place a regular withdrawal request on Kite for the MCX segment
Zerodha will process withdrawal requests you place on public holidays on the next trading day

title: How to select a bank account for withdrawal in Zerodha?
text:
When you withdraw funds, you can choose which bank account you want to receive the money: your primary or secondary bank accounts. Zerodha only pays out to bank accounts verified through a penny drop, which confirms that the account belongs to you.
What is a penny drop?
A penny drop is a verification process where Zerodha credits a nominal amount to your bank account to confirm that the account is valid and belongs to you. Only penny-drop-verified accounts are available for withdrawal.
If your bank account is not yet penny-drop-verified, you will see
Account verification is pending.
You will not be able to withdraw to this account until verification is complete.
To verify your bank account:
Visit
console.zerodha.com/account/bank.
Tap or click on the pencil icon.
Enter your bank account number and IFSC code, and accept the terms and conditions.
Select whether you want to receive the OTP via email or mobile, and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on
Verify.
Once verified, the account becomes available for withdrawal. There are no charges for penny-drop verification.
How to withdraw to a verified bank account
Open Kite app or Kite web and navigate to funds section.
Tap or click on
Withdraw.
Enter the amount and select the bank account you want to withdraw to: primary or secondary bank accounts.
Tap or click on
Confirm.
Why is a bank account not available for withdrawal?
A bank account may be unavailable for withdrawal if:
The penny drop verification has not been completed.
The bank account details on
Console
are outdated or incorrect.

title: Why is the payout amount less than what was requested?
text:
Your payout amount could be lower than your requested payout because your
withdrawable balance
can reduce after the end of day process when the charges and net obligation (any trading losses) for the day are debited from your account. The end of day process typically happens between 5 PM and 9 PM and is dependent on the time exchanges share the end of day files.
How withdrawal requests are processed
If you place a withdrawal request for an amount higher than your withdrawable balance, Zerodha will credit the available withdrawable balance to your bank account.
Example scenario
You place a withdrawal request for ₹12,000 at 10:00 AM
After the end of day processes, if your withdrawable amount is ₹9,000, Zerodha will credit this amount to your bank account
While the funds you deposit to your trading account during the day are available for trading immediately, you can only withdraw them the next day, owing to the end-of-day settlement and reconciliation processes, because of which your payout amount could be lower.

title: What is the withdrawable balance?
text:
Withdrawable balance is the amount you can transfer from your Zerodha account to your primary or secondary bank account.
This balance may differ from the net available funds shown in your funds statement because exchanges don't settle funds from equity and F&O trades instantly. Exchanges require
T+1 day to settle
funds from selling equities and F&O trades to your Zerodha account before you can withdraw them.
The withdrawable balance may change after market hours if you have traded during the day. This happens because Zerodha updates all charges and obligations after the end-of-day process between 5 PM and 9 PM.
Pledging stocks can also affect your withdrawable balance.
While you can use funds deposited to your trading account immediately for trading, you can only withdraw them the next day due to end-of-day settlement and reconciliation processes.

title: How to park your withdrawable balance in LIQUIDCASE ETF?
text:
You can invest your withdrawable balance directly into Zerodha's LIQUIDCASE ETF instead of withdrawing it to your bank account. This allows you to park your funds in a liquid security with relatively low risk and potentially better returns than keeping them in a savings bank account.
Zerodha Nifty 1D Rate Liquid ETF is Zerodha Fund House's Liquidcase that invests in low-risk overnight instruments like Treasury Bills and Repos. The ETF focuses on Net Asset Value (NAV) growth rather than dividend distribution.
You can sell LIQUIDCASE units anytime through Kite, as they trade like regular stocks on the exchange.
To invest your withdrawable balance in LIQUIDCASE ETF from Kite app:
Tap on your user ID.
Tap on
Funds.
Tap on
Withdraw.
Tap on
Invest in Zerodha AMC LIQUIDCASE
and enter the amount to invest.
Tap on
Continue
to place the order.
You can also invest your withdrawable balance in Zerodha AMC Liquidcase by visiting
console.zerodha.com/funds/overview.
Post-market and weekend orders
You can also place LIQUIDCASE investment orders after market hours. All orders placed after 3:30 PM, on trading holidays, and on weekends are collected and placed on Kite on the next trading day.
Things to keep in mind
You must enter an amount above the Last Traded Price (LTP) of the ETF to purchase at least 1 unit.
Zerodha calculates the maximum units you can purchase based on the ETF's LTP and the amount you enter.
Investing in LIQUIDCASE is treated as a regular investment. This means that regular
charges for Liquid ETFs
will be debited from your equity account on the trading day. The same charges apply when you sell.
To learn more about Zerodha’s Liquid case, visit
Zerodha Nifty 1D Rate Liquid ETF.

title: Why is the withdrawable balance negative?
text:
The withdrawable balance will be negative in the following scenarios:
Your
Zerodha account is in a debit balance.
Your funds are yet to be settled to your Zerodha account. In India, exchanges follow a rolling settlement cycle. Exchanges require T+1 day to settle funds from selling equities and F&O trades to your Zerodha account before you can withdraw them. Until then, the withdrawal amount is displayed in negative, indicating that it is still being processed for settlement. Your withdrawable balance becomes positive once the funds are settled.
Withdrawal requests placed when the withdrawal balance is negative will not be processed.

title: Why was my withdrawal request rejected?
text:
Your withdrawal request can be rejected for these reasons:
Unsettled trade funds:
You cannot withdraw funds from trades that are yet to be settled. Exchanges require
T+1 day
to settle funds from selling equities and F&O trades before you can withdraw them. For intraday and F&O trades, this includes realised profits, mark-to-market (M2M), or the sell value of options.
Same-day deposits:
You can trade with money added during the day immediately, but you cannot withdraw it on the same day. You can withdraw these funds after one day.¹
Payment gateway delays:
Money added through the payment gateway can take up to T+1 days to be credited to Zerodha. When it is not credited immediately, Zerodha uses its own capital to allow you to trade on T day, making same-day withdrawals not possible.²
What happens when you request more than your available balance
If you place a withdrawal request for an amount greater than your withdrawal balance, Zerodha will credit only the available
withdrawable balance
to your bank account.
Example scenario
Your withdrawable balance is ₹9000
You place a withdrawal request for ₹12,000
Zerodha will credit ₹9000 to your bank account
Notes
¹Zerodha processes withdrawal requests after the trading session, since it must consider obligations for all trades during the day. Due to potential issues with crediting funds, Zerodha processes withdrawal requests only after reconciling funds with respective banks. Banks share transaction details by early morning of the next day (T+1 day) due to the volume of transactions. After receiving transaction details, Zerodha reconciles them and processes fund withdrawals.
In some cases, Zerodha has received incorrect confirmation of fund credits from banks, and transactions have been reversed. To avoid such issues, Zerodha processes fund withdrawal requests on T+1 day.
²When you transfer funds through payment gateways, you receive credit on the same day. However, payment gateways settle the funds to Zerodha's account only on T+1 or T+2 days. Until then, Zerodha funds the margins for your trades from its own capital. Since Zerodha receives credit of funds transferred through payment gateways on T+1 or T+2 days, processing withdrawal requests of funds added on the same day would require funding them from Zerodha's own capital. This is why Zerodha cannot process withdrawal requests for funds added on the same day.

title: How much time does it take to process a withdrawal request?
text:
You can instantly withdraw money from your Zerodha account using the
Instant withdrawal feature.
Zerodha processes fund withdrawal requests at specific cut-off times. If you place your request before the cut-off time, your funds are typically credited to your bank account within 24 hours of the cut-off time. If you place your request after the cut-off time, it takes an additional working day (up to 48 hours in total) for your funds to be credited.
The withdrawal cut-off time for your account depends on whether your
Commodity segment
is activated.
If your Commodity segment is not activated
Weekdays (Mon–Fri): 10:00 PM
Saturday: 4:30 PM
Sunday & Public holidays: Requests are processed on the next working day
If your Commodity segment is activated
Weekdays (Mon–Fri): 11:59 PM
Saturday: 4:30 PM
Sunday & Public Holidays: Requests are processed on the next working day
Create a ticket
if you have not received your funds after the expected timeframe.
Things to keep in mind
The
Recent withdrawals
section includes a reference number for Equity payouts.
If you have not traded during the day, have no open positions, have not placed an instant withdrawal request, and have a sufficient balance on the withdrawal day, your withdrawal request will be processed on the same day. If you place your request before 5:00 PM, your money will be credited to your bank account on the same day. If you place your request after 5:00 PM, your money will be credited on the next day.
If you have placed CNC sell orders, your regular withdrawal request will only be processed at 10:00 PM.
If you have enabled
single ledger,
your fund withdrawal requests are processed only at 11:59 PM, irrespective of the segments.

title: When can I withdraw funds after selling shares or closing positions?
text:
You can withdraw funds from selling shares or closing positions after T+1 day, once the trades are settled and appear in your
withdrawable balance.
The settlement cycle for all instruments traded on Indian exchanges is T+1 day, where T stands for the trading day. This means you cannot withdraw the proceeds immediately after selling shares or closing F&O positions.
If you sell shares from your holdings or close F&O positions on Friday, you can withdraw the proceeds on Monday morning.

title: Are the funds for stock SIP deducted from the bank account?
text:
For stock SIPs, your funds are deducted from your Zerodha account. Your SIP order will fail if you do not have sufficient funds available on the specified date. You can use the
eMandate facility
to ensure your Zerodha account has the necessary funds.

title: Which banks support eMandates?
text:
You can find the complete list of banks supporting eMandates here
(WEB).
Banks with the
Netbanking
status as
Live
support eMandates.
The following banks are temporarily disabled from creating mandates:
Central Bank.
Standard Chartered.
Indian Overseas Bank (IOB).

title: How to cancel an eMandate?
text:
You can stop auto-debit of funds by cancelling the schedule instead of cancelling the entire eMandate. This allows you to use the same eMandate to set up schedules in the future without creating a new eMandate.
Cancel a schedule
You must cancel schedules at least 3 working days in advance from the next account credit date (4 working days for SBI accounts).
If you cancel a schedule after an upcoming debit is confirmed, the funds will be debited from your bank account and added to your Zerodha account. However, all future debits for that schedule will be cancelled. You can withdraw these funds from your Zerodha account if required.
Cancel the entire eMandate
An eMandate can also be completely cancelled. To cancel, visit
console.zerodha.com/funds/mandates
and follow the below steps:
Click on
Delete mandate.
Confirm the request.
3. Mandates that are under process show up with the
Pending Cancellation
status.
Your eMandate takes up to 5 working days to be deleted. You can
create a new mandate
once the old one is deleted.

title: Why is there a difficulty in setting up eMandate on iOS devices?
text:
This happens when your browser blocks pop-ups by default. You can
create eMandates
after enabling pop-ups by clicking on
Always show
in your iOS web browser.

title: When should the eMandate transaction be scheduled to ensure sufficient funds for stock SIP?
text:
You should schedule your eMandate transaction a few days before your stock SIP date because the bank confirmation of the eMandate transfer can be delayed. For example, if your stock SIP is scheduled for the 5th of every month, you should schedule your eMandate transaction for the 2nd or 3rd.
Your bank account is debited one working day before the eMandate scheduled date, and you receive SMS and email confirmations one day in advance.

title: Can eMandate be set up using a joint account?
text:
Some banks do not allow you to create eMandates from joint bank accounts. You can create standing instructions instead by adding Zerodha as a beneficiary using
Zerodha's bank account details
to automatically transfer funds on predefined dates from your netbanking portal.
Here is the process to create a standing instruction in HDFC Bank as an example:
Select Transfer within the bank.
Under Transfer type, select
Set Standing Instruction.
Select
Start Date, Frequency & End Date
as per your requirement.
Select Zerodha as Beneficiary and
Continue.

title: How to schedule an eMandate transaction?
text:
You can schedule fund transfers once you have created an
eMandate.
To schedule fund transfers, follow these steps:
Visit
console.zerodha.com/funds/mandates.
Specify the
Tag
name,
Account credit date,
Frequency,
and the
Amount
under
Set new schedule.
If your eMandate transaction fails due to insufficient funds, the fund transfer will not be retried. You will need to
transfer funds manually to your Zerodha account.
The maximum limit per schedule is ₹1 lakh. Multiple schedules can be created in a single day, up to a cumulative limit of ₹1 crore.

title: How to set up automatic fund transfers from a bank account to a Zerodha trading account?
text:
You can use the eMandate facility to automatically transfer up to ₹1 crore at pre-set schedules from your bank account to your Zerodha account. You cannot use current bank accounts to create eMandates. Zerodha does not charge for eMandate registrations or transactions. However, if a transaction fails due to insufficient funds, your bank may charge a penalty.
Creating an eMandate
Follow these steps to create an eMandate:
Visit
console.zerodha.com/funds/mandates
and click on
Create a new mandate.
Select the account for which the mandate is to be created.
Authenticate the bank account using a debit card or netbanking.
Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the process on the bank’s mandate approval gateway.
Once the mandate is registered, create a schedule to transfer funds to the Zerodha account.
It may take up to 5 days for the bank to activate the mandate.
Creating a schedule
Follow these steps to create a schedule:
Name the schedule.
Select fund transfer date.
Select frequency and the amount to be transferred.
Click on
Create schedule.

text:
There are no charges for eMandate registrations or transaction costs for transferring funds using eMandate at Zerodha. However, your bank may charge you a penalty if your transaction fails due to insufficient funds.
Check with the bank for the latest charges.

title: Why did my e-Mandate registration fail?
text:
Your e-Mandate registration fails when the authentication using net banking or your debit card is unsuccessful. This happens if you close the authentication window before entering your credentials or if you enter incorrect credentials. Zerodha cannot determine the specific reason for the failure; only your bank has this information. If your mandate creation fails, you can
recreate it from Console.
Banks do not support e-Mandates for customers using current, joint, and NRE-PIS
bank accounts. If you cannot set up your e-Mandate successfully on Console, you can place standing instructions to automatically transfer money through your bank's net banking portal by adding Zerodha's account as a beneficiary.

title: What is the daily limit for eMandate transactions?
text:
You can transfer up to ₹1 crore per day through eNACH mandates. If you need to transfer more than ₹1 crore in a single day, you can transfer manually using
other available methods.
Setting up scheduled transfers
Once you receive approval for your mandate, you can
create a schedule
that specifies the exact amount and date for debiting money from your account. You can create multiple schedules as long as your total daily debits do not exceed the maximum limit of ₹1 crore.

title: Why does money get debited from my bank account before the scheduled eMandate transaction day?
text:
Your bank debits the money before the scheduled eMandate transaction date to ensure the funds are available in your trading account on the specified date.
Why this happens
eMandate transfers take one working day to reflect in your trading account. Your bank debits the money during banking hours, but the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) credits Zerodha's bank accounts and confirms the transaction at the end of the day. Your bank therefore debits the funds at least one working day in advance so the money appears in your trading account on the transaction date.

title: Why is my eMandate registration pending for more than 5 working days?
text:
Your eMandate registration typically completes within 5 working days. However, sometimes Zerodha does not receive confirmation from the bank on the registration, and the mandate remains in a pending status on Console.
Since your bank must provide the confirmation, Zerodha cannot provide a timeline for successful verification. However, Zerodha periodically follows up with the respective banks through its channels in such cases.

title: How to change email ID or mobile number registered with the AMC for Coin investments?
text:
The email ID and mobile number registered with the AMC are the same as those registered with Zerodha. These details are also captured by Central Depository Services (India) Limited (CDSL), the depository, and shared with the Registrar and Transfer agents (RTA). You can change your email ID or mobile number registered with the AMC by
updating these details with Zerodha.
Things to keep in mind
The RTAs update their database every weekend.
Your Coin investments are held in your demat account, so the
nominee(s) added to your Zerodha account
also apply to your Coin investments.

title: Why are my investments made on Coin not visible on the AMC website?
text:
Your investments made via Coin cannot be seen on the AMC website for these reasons:
Your investments made on Coin are in demat mode, and the AMC website only displays investments made in the folio mode
Your
mobile number or email is not updated with the RTA,
which prevents you from logging in

title: Can I switch between two different schemes of the same AMC on Coin?
text: You cannot directly switch between two different schemes of the same AMC on Coin. However, you can redeem one fund and purchase another fund within the same AMC. Even though the funds are not credited to your bank account, capital gains tax is still applicable.

title: Why can't I log in to the AMC website to check my investments?
text:
Your mutual fund investments made on Coin are held in demat form and may not be visible on the AMC website. AMCs have discretion over whether to show investments made in demat form on their websites.
You can track your investments through:
Coin:
View your mutual fund investments directly on Coin.
CDSL demat holding statements:
Download the
statement from CDSL Easi.
Did you know?
ELSS statement can be downloaded on Coin.

title: How to check the realised profit and loss of mutual fund investments on Coin?
text:
You can check the realised profit and loss for mutual fund investments by following these steps:
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Reports
and then on
Tax P&L.
Select the
Financial year
and the
quarter range.
Click on the
arrow button.
Click on
Download Tax P&L
report for all segments.

title: Why does the mutual fund NAV display T-2 day value on Console?
text:
Your mutual fund NAV displayed on Console reflects the T-2 day value because the NAV announcement timings vary depending on the type of scheme.
NAV announcement timings by scheme type
Equity scheme
9 PM on trading day (T day)
Liquid scheme
11 PM on T day
International underlying scheme
1:30 PM on T+1 day
Here's how the NAV data reaches Console:
Data vendors receive the data from AMFI and update it on T+1 day in the morning.
The Bhav copy for mutual funds is generated by T+1 day at 3 PM.
Console uses this Bhav copy to calculate your mutual fund P&L on the night of T+1 day.
On T+2 day, your holdings on Console reflect the NAV as of T day and provide an accurate representation of your holdings.
You can track your holding data for mutual funds as per T-1 day's NAV on Coin.

title: Why is the current mutual fund value on Console based on the T-2 days NAV?
text:
The current mutual fund value on Console is based on the T-2 days NAV for the following reasons:
For most Mutual Fund schemes, the AMC officially declares the NAV (Net Asset Value) at the end of the trading day, usually around 11 PM. This information is then transmitted to AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India), which updates its data by midnight or sometimes as late as 3-4 AM on the following day, which we refer to as T+1 day.
For different types of schemes:
For Equity Schemes, the NAV is announced by 9 PM on the same trading day (T-day).
For Liquid Schemes, the NAV is announced by 11 PM on T-day.
For schemes with international assets, the NAV is announced by 12 PM on T+1 day.
Additionally, for international schemes, a report known as the Bhav copy, containing daily trading data for Mutual Funds, is generated by T+1 day at 3 PM. Console uses this Bhav copy to calculate your Mutual Fund Profit and Loss (P&L) on the evening of T+1 day.
As a result, by T+2 day, the information Console displays accurately represents your Mutual Fund's value as of T-day. This approach ensures that you receive the most precise and up to date representation of your holdings.
You can track your holdings data for mutual funds as per T-1 days NAV on Coin.

title: Why is the buy average for some mutual funds shown as a discrepancy?
text:
Console displays your buy average with a discrepant symbol in the following cases:
If you transferred mutual funds from another demat account to Zerodha, but the system has not updated the original purchase price.
If you dematerialised the mutual funds.
You will see a discrepant symbol in the following places:
Coin dashboard
Under the
Holdings
section on Coin.
On Console
How to fix the discrepancy
To fix the discrepancy, follow these steps:
Visit
Console.
Click on
Portfolio
and then
Holdings.
Click on
Options
and then on
View discrepancy.
Click on the fund and then on
Add trade.
Enter the
Purchase date, Avg price, Qty and Type as others.
In case of a partial discrepancy, the transactions that are already available in the MF trade book will show up under the
View transactions
option. If a few discrepant entries are added, it is displayed on Coin as shown below:
The buy average will be calculated as per the price entered and will be displayed under holdings.
How to edit or delete a discrepant entry
Follow these steps:
Click on
Options.
To edit,
click on
Edit trade
and make the required changes.
To delete, click on
Delete trade.
The discrepant entry can only be edited or deleted while the status is
Pending.
Once the recalculation is complete, the trade entry cannot be edited or deleted and the buy average will be permanently set.
The average price for the holdings will be calculated based on the First In First Out (FIFO) principle after considering the discrepant entry. The buy average is updated within 24 working hours after the discrepant entry is made.
Did you know?
Newly allotted units may have the buy average as NA.

title: How to convert my regular mutual fund investment to direct mutual fund?
text:
Transferring your regular mutual funds to Zerodha does not convert them to direct mutual funds.
You cannot convert regular mutual funds to direct mutual funds. However, you can redeem your regular mutual funds and invest in the direct mutual fund of the same plan.

title: How to transfer mutual funds from other platform to Coin?
text:
If you transfer your holdings from another broker to Zerodha,
Zerodha refunds the transfer charges your current broker deducts,
up to ₹30 per stock, capped at ₹500 in total. This isn't just for new accounts. If you already have a Zerodha account and are moving stocks from another broker, we'll refund those charges too.
You must cancel any active SIPs in the scheme before transferring mutual fund units to Zerodha. Once you transfer the units, you can create an SIP on Coin.
The transfer process depends on how you hold your units:
Units held in demat mode
CDSL account
You can follow the transfer process mentioned in
How to transfer shares from the other broker to Zerodha?
NSDL account
Submit the following documents to your NSDL DP (the other broker):
Obtain a Delivery Instruction Slip (DIS) from the other broker
On the DIS, mention the number of units and the Zerodha demat account number. For the demat account number, visit
console.zerodha.com/account/demat.
Submit the DIS to the other broker.
Example of DIS
Units held in physical mode or in Statement of Account (SOA)
The mutual fund units must be
dematerialised or destatementized.
Units under lock-in period
The only way you can transfer ELSS mutual funds under lock-in from one demat to another is via
closure cum transfer process.
You can only move the locked-in units to another demat account of the same account holder. If your ELSS scheme is out of the lock-in period or has free units, you can transfer it without any restrictions.
Processing time:
After you submit all documents, the transfer takes up to 4 days to complete.
Things to keep in mind
Your mutual fund holdings will reflect as discrepant after you transfer them to Zerodha.You should
manually enter the buy average
for such units.
If you hold regular mutual funds in demat or physical form, you should exit those funds and repurchase them on Coin. Transferring regular mutual funds to Zerodha will not convert them to direct mutual funds.
If the other broker asks for it, you can get your
CMR copy from Console.

title: How to fill up the mutual fund dematerialisation form?
text:
To fill out the dematerialisation form
(PDF),
follow these steps:
Enter the
Date.
Ignore the
DRF No.
field.
In the
