continuous chart feature.
You cannot access historical data for expired options contracts on Kite.

title: How are the pivot points derived while using the Central Pivot Range (CPR)?
text:
The Central Pivot Range (CPR) is an indicator to identify key price points to set up trades. The CPR consists of three components:
Pivot
Bottom Central Pivot (BC)
Top Central Pivot (TC)
These are derived from the underlying high, low, and close price calculations:
Pivot = (High + Low + Close) ÷ 3.
Bottom CPR = (High + Low) ÷ 2.
Top CPR = (Pivot - BC) + Pivot.
The OHLC reference period for each chart timeframe is as follows:
Previous day candle HLC values are considered for 1, 3, 5, 10, 15 min timeframe.
Previous week candle HLC values are considered for 30 min and 1 hour.
Previous month candle HLC values are considered for 1 day timeframe.
Previous year candle HLC values are considered for weekly/monthly timeframe
Example Scenario
The chart of SBIN on 24/08/2022 (the previous day) has it's Open (O) - 519, High (H) - 523, Low (L) - 512.3, and Close (C) - 519.3.
On 25/08/2022, the pivot point data was 518.53. i.e. (H+L+C ÷ 3)
Here if the pivot point data is ascertained from the OHLC data of 25/08/2022, it will not match with the pivot points as it is calculated using the previous day’s OHLC.
The formula for calculating pivot points is mentioned below, with an example on Kite ChartIQ.
Assume XYZ’s stock has its High (H) - 129.7, Low (L) - 126.9 and Close (C) - 127.35, respectively. So the pivot points in Kite would be calculated as follows:
KITE(ChartIQ)
Pivot point = (H + L + C) / 3
127.98
First support level (S1) = (2 * P) – H
126.27
Second support level (S2) = P – (H – L)
125.18
Third support level (S3) = P – 2*(H-L)
122.38
First resistance level (R1) = (2 * P) – L
129.07
Second resistance level (R2) = P + (H – L)
130.78
Third resistance level (R3) = P + 2*(H – L)
133.58
Did you know?
CPR helps the trader identify key price points and the associated trend around these price points. Today’s CPR values act as the reference for tomorrow’s trading.
To learn more about CPR visit
zerodha.com/varsity/chapter/the-central-pivot-range.

title: How to deactivate your Zerodha account?
text:
You can deactivate your account if you intend to stop trading temporarily and resume it later. This can be done using
Kill Switch.
If you are closing your Zerodha account because of employer restrictions, you can request
Zerodha to be empanelled as a registered broker with your company.
What happens when you deactivate your account
When you deactivate your account:
Your trading account will be deactivated, and a freeze will be applied on your demat. No credits or debits can happen to your demat account
You will have no access to Kite and Console
AMC and other such charges will continue to be levied on your trading ledger, if applicable
You will continue to receive periodic emails like account statements, holding statements, etc.
How to deactivate your account
To deactivate your account, download and print the Account Closure/Deactivation Form
(PDF).
You can refer to the Sample copy
(PDF).
Tick on
Deactivate account,
fill up the form and courier it to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Alternatively, you can
digitally sign (eSign)
the filled account closure form and submit it by
creating a ticket.
The account closure form will be processed within 72 working hours.
To reactivate the account, print the reactivation letter
(PDF),
fill in the details, sign it, and courier it along with a self-attested copy of the PAN card and address proof. Sample copy of the reactivation letter
(PDF).
Address proof can be any one of the following: Driving Licence, Voter’s ID, Passport, Aadhaar. If an Aadhaar is being submitted as proof, ensure that a
masked Aadhaar
is submitted.

title: How to update the income details for the Zerodha account?
text:
You can update your income proof by following these steps:
Visit
account.zerodha.com/account.
Click on
Login
and then on
Income details.
Select your
Annual income.
Select
Income Proof (Optional)¹.
Accept the undertaking.
Click on
Continue.
Income proof will be updated within 48 working hours of completing the process, and Zerodha will send an email to the registered email address notifying the same.
Zerodha may request clients to update their income details via email or Kite notification for the following reasons:
As part of the Prevention of Money Laundering (PMLA) surveillance, if your transactions in your Zerodha account exceed the income details you declared earlier, you must update your income details to continue trading or investing.
Periodic review of clients' financial information
(WEB)
and proofs
(PDF)
as per SEBI guidelines to allow continued trading in the derivatives segment.
Yearly Update of Income Details for
Clients of Special Category (CSC)
as part of enhanced due diligence.
Politically Exposed Person (PEPs) must submit income proof while opening an account due to their classification as
Clients of Special Category (CSC).
Notes
¹Submitting income proof is optional. However, to continue trading in F&O, income proof must be submitted, or the F&O segment will be disabled. Any one of the following documents can be submitted as income proof:
A Bank statement with the bank logo and seal in the name of the Zerodha account holder for the last 6 months with an average balance of more than ₹10,000.
Latest salary slip with gross monthly income exceeding ₹15,000.
Latest ITR acknowledgement with gross annual income exceeding ₹1,20,000.
Latest Form 16 with gross annual income exceeding ₹1,20,000.
Certificate of net worth more than ₹10,00,000.
Latest statement of demat holdings with current holdings value exceeding ₹10,000. (Your holdings must not be pledged)
Latest Fixed Deposit (FD) receipt with a deposit amount exceeding ₹1,00,000.

title: How to deactivate F&O, Commodity, or Currency segment?
text:
You can deactivate your F&O (Futures and Options), Commodity, or Currency segment in two ways:
Temporary deactivation
You can temporarily disable your F&O segment using the
Kill switch feature.
This keeps your segment disabled until you choose to enable it again. Before you disable a segment, ensure you have no open orders or positions. Once you disable a segment, you can re-enable it only after 12 hours.
Deactivation through account modification
You can deactivate your F&O segment by submitting an account modification form:
For individual accounts:
Fill the account modification form
(WEB).
E-sign
the form.
Submit the form by
creating a ticket.
For non-individual accounts:
Fill the account modification form
(WEB).
Add your company seal and authorised signature.
Send the hard copy to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Your F&O segment will be deactivated within 48 working hours. Once
it is deactivated, you’ll need to complete the
F&O activation process to activate it again
.
Things to keep in mind
Once the GTT triggers, if you have disabled the segment, your orders will be rejected. The same applies to basket orders and SIPs that get triggered.
Even if you disable the segment, your demat account remains open with Zerodha. Hence, you will still be charged
Account Maintenance Charge (AMC).

title: How to update PAN details on an existing Zerodha account?
text:
NSE does not allow PAN to be updated in trading accounts. You can only update your PAN in your demat account. You must then close your existing trading account and open a new trading and demat account with your new PAN.
Zerodha charges ₹25 + 18% GST as account modification charges. The process takes up to 10 working days after Zerodha receives and verifies your documents.
How does the PAN update process work?
Update your PAN in your existing demat account and close your trading account.
Open a new trading and demat account
with your new PAN.
Transfer your holdings from your old demat account to your new demat account through
closure cum transfer.
Required documents
Type of document
Requirements
Account modification and KYC forms
Account modification form
(PDF)
with signature in the F box. On the KYC page, affix the latest passport-size photograph, sign across the photo in the F1 box, and sign in the F2 box.
PAN
Self-attested copy of the PAN with the signature under
Applicant signature.
Address proof
Self-attested copy of address proof.  Any one of the following documents:
Driving licence
Voter ID
Passport
National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA) job card
National Population Register (NPR) letter
Masked Aadhaar card
Income Tax Department (ITD) Intimation letter
Intimation letter for deletion/deactivation of old PAN from the Income Tax Department (ITD).
Handwritten letter
A handwritten and self-attested letter specifying the reasons for cancellation of the old PAN, with the old and new PAN numbers mentioned.
Account closure form
Download and complete the account closure form
(PDF)
and tick the trading account option.
Ensure your signature on all forms matches the
signature you provided when opening your account.
Courier all documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
What happens next
Once Zerodha receives your documents, Zerodha will update your PAN in your demat account and close your trading account. You can then open a new trading and demat account with your new PAN and initiate a closure cum transfer to move your holdings.

corporate

title: Why is the error "Mobile number updation cannot be processed online for your account. Please initiate request offline." displayed?
text:
You see the error
Mobile number updation cannot be processed online for your account. Please initiate request offline
if you have a non-individual account, joint account or do not have a demat account with Zerodha. You can
change the contact details offline in Zerodha.

title: Why is the error "Nominee addition cannot be processed online for your account. Please initiate request offline." displayed?
text:
You see the error
Nominee addition cannot be processed online for your account. Please initiate request offline
if you have a joint account, non-individual account or do not have a demat account with Zerodha.
For joint accounts
To add a nominee to a joint account, all the account holders must sign the nomination form
(PDF)
and courier it to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
You can refer to the sample copy of the nomination form
(PDF).
For non-individual accounts
As per the CDSL guidelines
(PDF),
the nomination facility is available only for individual demat accounts. Therefore, non-individual account holders cannot add a nominee.
Clients who do not have a demat account with Zerodha cannot add a nominee.
Things to keep in mind
Your nominees' names must match their documents to facilitate easier claims in adverse situations.
If your nominee is a minor, mention the guardian's details and provide ID proof of the guardian. You cannot add the account holder as the nominee's guardian.
If you have a minor account, you cannot add the guardian as the nominee.
Your nominee would apply to your trading and demat account, including mutual fund investments made through Coin.

title: How to remove password protection from a PDF document?
text:
You can remove password protection from a PDF document by following these steps:
Enter the password and open the PDF.
Click on
Menu.
Click on
Print.
Select
Print to PDF
and click on
Print.
Select the folder, enter the file name and click on
Save.
Your PDF document will be saved without password protection.

box. Ensure that your signature on the documents matches

Segments cannot be enabled online for your account. Please initiate request offline
if you have a non-individual account, joint account, NRI account, minor account or do not have a demat account with Zerodha.
Non-individual, joint and non-Zerodha demat accounts
You can enable segments offline if you have a non-individual account, joint account or do not have a demat account with Zerodha by following these steps:
Print the segment addition form
(PDF).
Fill out the form and sign.
Courier the segment addition form and an income proof
1
to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076

(PDF)
and HUF sample copy
(PDF).

For currency segment:
To enable the currency segment,
courier the signed RBI declaration form
(PDF)
along with the segment addition form and income proof¹.
NRI accounts:
If you are an NRI client, you can
activate the F&O segment online through Kite.
Minors:
You cannot enable F&O if you have a minor account as you cannot place intraday and F&O orders.
Note
1:

The latest six months' bank statement or passbook of the company account.

DP holding statement of the entity.

title: What is the procedure for changing the signature at Zerodha?
text:
You can change your signature by couriering the required documents or by visiting the Zerodha Customer Support Centre. You can
view your signature, which you uploaded during the account opening process, through Console
You need the following documents if you are couriering them or handing them over at any branch office:
Account modification form
(PDF):
The following have to be filled in the account modification form:
Details: Change of signature.
Type of change: Modification.
Existing details: Old signature
New details: New signature.
New signature
in the
F
box.
Banker's letter
(PDF):
The letter must have a passport-size photo with the new signature across it. The old and new signatures of the client and the banker's seal and signature.
Self-attested copy of the PAN card.
Self-attested copy of the address proof. The address proof can be any one of the following:  Driving licence, Voter ID, Passport or Aadhaar. If an Aadhaar is being submitted as proof, ensure that a
masked Aadhaar
is submitted.
Submission process
Prepare all documents listed above.
Email soft copies of the forms to
[email protected]
for review.
Wait for the review process to complete and any necessary corrections to be identified.
Courier the documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
You can check the sample account modification, KYC forms
(PDF)
and banker letter
(PDF)
for reference
If you visit the head office, you need the following documents:
Account modification form
(PDF),
which must be signed in the presence of a Zerodha employee.
Self-attested copy of the PAN card.
Self-attested copy of the address proof. The address proof can be any of the following: Driving licence, Voter ID, Passport or Aadhaar. If an Aadhaar is being submitted as proof, ensure that a
masked Aadhaar
is submitted.
Charges:
Modification charges of ₹25 + 18% GST are applicable.
Processing time:
Your signature will be updated within 72 working hours.

title: How to update the Date of Birth (DOB), gender, marital status, occupation or PEP status on the Zerodha account?
text:
You can update your DOB and gender online if you have updated the correct details on your Aadhaar and Income Tax Database (ITD). You can also update your marital status, occupation and PEP status by completing the KYC process.
Online process
Visit
account.zerodha.com/account.
Click on
Login
and log in to Kite.
Click on
Complete Rekyc.
Select the segments you want to activate and click on
Continue.
Update your contact details if required and click on
Continue.
Update your profile details, including your annual income, and click on
Continue.
Click on
Update Address
and then on
Continue.
Click on
Continue
and enter your
Aadhar number.
Enter captcha and click on
Next.
Enter the OTP and click on
Continue.
Enter your 6-digit security PIN and click on
Continue.
Update your bank account if required and click on
Continue.
Complete the FATCA declaration and click on
Continue.
Upload income proof to trade in F&O (optional).
Complete the In-Person Verification (IPV).
Click on
Sign now
and complete E-sign by entering your Aadhar and OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on verify OTP.
Offline process
If your Aadhaar is not linked to your mobile number or if you have a joint account and have not updated the correct details, you need to fill out the account modification and KYC form
(PDF)
and send it along with a self-attested copy of the following:
PAN card.
Driving Licence, Voter ID, Passport or Aadhaar card as address proof.
If an Aadhaar is being submitted as proof, ensure that a
masked Aadhaar
is submitted.
Courier all the documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Things to keep in mind
An account modification charge of ₹25
+ 18% GST is applicable for updating the details. The changes will be updated within 72 working hours.
The name on the Aadhaar must match the name in the digital signature (e-sign). If they differ, the
e-signed forms
will be rejected.

title: How to update the name while e-signing through Digio?
text:
If you are opening a minor account, the guardian must e-sign the forms using the guardian's Aadhaar. If you use the minor's Aadhaar instead, you will see an error message. If you are using the guardian's Aadhaar and still face the error, you need to update the name before e-signing.
You need to ensure the name on your Aadhaar matches the name in your digital signature (e-sign). If they differ, your e-signed forms will be rejected.
How to update your name during e-signing
Follow these steps to update your name while e-signing on Digio:
Click on
Change name and Proceed.
Enter the Aadhaar number.
Click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP.
Click on
Verify OTP.
Once you update your name, you can continue e-signing the document.
If you do not want to change your name, you can click on Proceed and complete the e-sign process.

title: How to change the name in my Zerodha account?
text:
You can change the name in your Zerodha account by first updating your name with the Income Tax Department (ITD) and then completing the process
online
via re-KYC. You need your Aadhaar linked to your mobile number to complete the process online. If your Aadhaar is not linked to your mobile number, use the
offline
process instead.
Zerodha updates the name on your account from the ITD records, not from the documents you submitted when opening your account. If your name differs between Zerodha and your submitted documents, you must first
update the name with the ITD
before raising a request with Zerodha.
Online process
Visit
account.zerodha.com
and complete the re-KYC process.
If your name on Zerodha records does not match the ITD records, you will see a prompt to enter your name as per the ITD.
Complete Aadhaar authentication.
Upload the relevant supporting document depending on the type of request:
For minor name changes, upload your PAN or passport.
As per CDSL guidelines, for major name changes (due to marriage, divorce, personal preference, or removal of names), ensure the following before starting the online process:
The name change is either published in the Gazette or supported by a marriage certificate with a verifiable QR code.
The supporting document is verifiable online via the relevant government source (e.g., the Maharashtra Gazette).
Complete the e-sign.
The submitted document will be reviewed, verified against the relevant government source, and approved if it meets the validation requirements.
Offline process
Documents required:
Account modification form and KYC
(PDF),
Sample copy
(PDF)
Annexure B
(PDF),
Sample copy
(PDF).
(Required for: minor name change, name change due to marriage or divorce, change in name for any other reason, removal of middle and last name)
PAN: Self-attested copy of the PAN with the signature in the Applicant signature box.
Address proof: Self-attested copy of the address proof. If you submit an Aadhaar as proof, ensure you submit a
masked Aadhaar
Additional documents for name change due to marriage or divorce, change in name for any other reason, or removal of middle and last name (any one of the following):
Marriage or divorce certificate: a notarised copy of the marriage or divorce certificate
Passport: self-attested copy of the passport showing the husband's or father's name
Publication: if you reside in Karnataka or Punjab, provide an original newspaper publication or a notarised photocopy. For all others, provide an original notarised copy of the name change publication in the official gazette. The old or existing name on the gazette must match the name on the PAN you submitted while opening your account with Zerodha. Sample copy
(PDF)
You do not have to fill in the address on the KYC or modification forms. If you wish to fill them in before sending,
create a ticket
with the soft copies so the team can verify all documents are in place before you courier.
Your signature on these documents must match the
signature you provided when opening your account.
Your account modification form and supporting documents are valid for 30 days from the submission date.
Courier your documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Your name will be updated within 72 working hours of Zerodha receiving your forms or approving your online submission. A ₹25 account modification charge, plus GST, applies.

192A 4th Floor,

change your address online if your mobile number is linked to your Aadhaar.

If you are an NRI account holder, you must provide a notarised copy of your address proof.
Your account modification form and supporting documents stay valid for 30 days from the submission date.

title: Why am I not receiving SMS and email trade alerts from the exchanges?
text:
You will not receive SMS and email trade alerts from the exchanges when your contact details registered with Zerodha are incorrect. You can
update your contact details with Zerodha through online or offline process.
If your contact details are correct and you still do not receive trade alerts, you can write to the exchanges requesting alerts for your trades.The contact details for the exchanges are as follows:
NSE:
nseindia.com/contact/corporate-office
BSE:
bseindia.com/static/about/contact_us.aspx
MCX:
mcxindia.com/contact-us/department-contacts
Did you know?
You may not receive trade alerts if your mobile number is on DND (Do Not Disturb).
The trades can be verified on the exchange websites.

title: Why was an email with the updated Client Master Report (CMR) sent by Zerodha?
text:
You receive an email with the updated
Client Master Report (CMR)
for the following reasons:
You opened a new demat account with Zerodha.
You recently modified your account details, such as a name change, address change, mobile number change, email change, updating income details or any other change in your demat account.
Your demat account was converted to a
BSDA
or a NON-BSDA.
As per the SEBI circular
(PDF),
you receive the updated CMR whenever your demat account is mapped or unmapped from a trading account.
You closed your Zerodha account.
The validity of the updated CMR link is mentioned in the email you received.

title: What is Client Master Report (CMR), and how to get it?
text:
A Client Master Report (CMR) is a digitally signed PDF certificate issued by a broker to a client with the details of their demat account, such as demat ID, date of birth, bank details, nomination details etc.
You can use your CMR copy to
transfer stocks¹ in your demat account from one broker to another.
How to get your CMR copy
Follow these steps to get your CMR copy:
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Documents.
Select
Zerodha CMR copy.
Click on
E-mail to me.
Your CMR will be available for download only after 48 working hours of your Zerodha account being opened. Similarly, any modifications made to your CMR will reflect, and the updated CMR will be available for download after 48 working hours of the modification request.
You can also request the hard copy of the CMR by
creating a ticket.
The first CMR hard copy is free. Additional copies cost ₹20, plus ₹100 for courier charges and 18% GST.
Note
¹As per NSDL
(PDF)
and CDSL
(PDF)
regulations,
a digitally signed CMR is as good as a physical CMR for off-market and online transfers. To transfer shares via closure cum transfer from your previous broker to Zerodha, you submit the closure form and Zerodha CMR copy to your previous broker.

title: What are DP name, DP ID, BO ID and demat ID?
text:
DP name:
Depository Participant (DP) name is Zerodha Broking Limited. The DP is a member of the depository. The demat account is opened with the depository (CDSL) through the DP (Zerodha).
DP ID:
The DP ID is the identification number of the Depository Participant (DP) where the demat account is maintained. The DP ID is the same for all Zerodha customers. It is 12081600 or 12081601.
BO ID:
The Beneficiary Owner Identification Number (BO ID) is an 8-digit number used to identify a unique demat account held with the depository (CDSL) through your  Depository Participant (DP).
Demat ID: The demat ID is a 16-digit number. It is a combination of the DP ID and the BO ID. It is required when applying for IPOs and
generating the CDSL TPIN for holdings authorisation.
You can view the DP ID, BO ID, and Demat ID by following these steps:
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Demat.

Depository Participant (DP) name is Zerodha Broking Limited. The DP is a member of the depository. The demat account is opened with the depository (CDSL) through the DP (Zerodha).

The DP ID is the identification number of the Depository Participant (DP) where the demat account is maintained. The DP ID is the same for all Zerodha customers. It is 12081600 or 12081601.
BO ID:
The Beneficiary Owner Identification Number (BO ID) is an 8-digit number used to identify a unique demat account held with the depository (CDSL) through your  Depository Participant (DP).
Demat ID: The demat ID is a 16-digit number. It is a combination of the DP ID and the BO ID. It is required when applying for IPOs and
generating the CDSL TPIN for holdings authorisation.
You can view the DP ID, BO ID, and Demat ID by following these steps:
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Demat.

title: How to add a nominee to the Zerodha account online?
text:
You can add nominees to your Zerodha account online if your mobile number is linked to Aadhaar. You can add up to 3 nominees to your account.
Steps to add a nominee online
Log in to
Console.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Nominees.
Click on
Add nominee.
Up to 3 nominees can be added.
Enter the nominee details such as name, contact details
(Rediffmail isn't allowed),
address, PAN (mandatory), etc. If multiple nominees are being added, select the percentage of share accordingly. If a single nominee is added, enter the percentage of share as 100%. If the account holder, nominee or guardian has a middle name, enter it along with the first name.
Click on
Continue.
Agree to the
Terms and Conditions
and click
on Proceed.
Click on
Sign now.
Accept the terms and conditions, enter the Aadhaar number of the account holder and click on
Send OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on
Verify OTP.
Zerodha will send you an email notification once the process is completed, and your nominee will be added to your account within 5 working days.
If your mobile number is not linked to Aadhaar, follow the
offline process to add a nominee.
If
Invalid Pincode
error is displayed while completing the online process, see
Why is the Invalid Pincode error displayed while adding a nominee online?
Things to keep in mind
Your nominees' names must match their documents to facilitate easier claims in adverse situations
If Zerodha deactivates your account due to
inactivity
for 24 consecutive months and you do not complete Re-KYC within 30 days, Zerodha will send an
email
to your nominees.
You cannot add nominees to non-individual accounts
If your nominee is a minor, mention the guardian's details. You cannot be added as the guardian of your nominee
For minor accounts, you cannot add the guardian as the nominee
Your nominees apply to your trading and demat account, including mutual fund investments made through Coin
If you have a dormant account, you must
reactivate your account
to add a nominee.
Joint account holders can only add nominees offline
NRIs can add nominees online, provided their Aadhaar is linked to their active mobile number
Charitable trusts cannot be added as a nominee

title: How to add a nominee(s) to the Zerodha account offline?
text:
You can
add a nominee online
if your mobile number is linked to Aadhaar and you have not added a nominee earlier.
To add nominees to the Zerodha account offline, courier the nomination form
(PDF)
to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Sample copy of the
nomination form
(PDF).
Nominee details will be updated within 5 working days after Zerodha receives the forms.
If you need to add an additional nominee to your account, you must send the account modification form
(PDF)
and the nominee form
(PDF).
In the account modification form, mention your existing nominee under the
Existing details
section, and mention both your existing nominee and the new nominee you want to add under the
New details
section. You can also
update or modify nominee details in Zerodha.
Things to keep in mind
Your nominees' names must match their documents to facilitate easier claims in adverse situations
If Zerodha deactivates your account due to
inactivity
for 24 consecutive months and you do not complete Re-KYC within 30 days, Zerodha will send an
email
to your nominees.
You cannot add nominees to non-individual accounts
If your nominee is a minor, mention the guardian details and provide ID proof of the guardian. You cannot be added as the guardian of your nominee
For minor accounts, you cannot add the guardian as the nominee
Your nominee applies to your trading and demat account, including mutual fund investments made through Coin
If you have a dormant account, you must
reactivate your account
to add a nominee.
You cannot add charitable trusts as nominees
Joint account holders can only add nominees offline
NRIs can add nominees online, provided their Aadhaar is linked to their active mobile number

title: Why is the error "Invalid Pincode" displayed while adding a nominee online?
text:
Zerodha uses the pincodes available on the Indian Post website to avoid exchange rejections. The
Invalid pincode
error is displayed when the pincode entered is unavailable on the Indian Post website. If the pincode is linked to a different city, that city name is fetched and displayed. To check the pincodes updated on the Indian Post website, visit
indiapost.gov.in/vas/pages/LocatePostOffices.aspx.
Did you know?
Zerodha does not send any postal communication to the nominees. Clients can choose a pincode of a neighbouring post office.

title: Why has the exchange or depository sent an email asking to add a nominee to the Zerodha account?
text:
As per the SEBI circular
(WEB),
exchanges and depositories
(PDF)
must ask clients to opt-in or out of a nomination for their accounts. Clients are not required to act because Zerodha has communicated their preferences to the exchanges, and their trading account will remain unaffected.
Zerodha strongly recommends adding a nominee(s) to the account, as it is critical in planning seamless succession. To learn how to add a nominee(s), see
How to add a nominee to the Zerodha account online?
To learn if a nominee has been added to the account, visit
console.zerodha.com/account/nominee.
Clients who have not added nominee(s) or do not wish to add a nominee(s) to their account can ignore the email.

title: Can an NRI be added as a nominee to a resident Zerodha account?
text:
Yes, an NRI can be added as a nominee to a resident Zerodha account. To add an NRI as a nominee, the NRI's address and mobile number must be Indian. Clients are not required to provide address proof while adding a nominee.
To learn how to add nominee(s) online, see
How to add a nominee to the Zerodha account online?
Did you know?
A resident can also be added as a nominee to an NRI Zerodha account.

title: How to update or modify the nominee details in Zerodha?
text:
You can
add a nominee online
if your mobile number is linked to your Aadhaar and you have not added a nominee earlier.
You can modify certain nominee details online or delete nominees offline by submitting the required forms to Zerodha.
Online
You can modify the following nominee details online:
Name change/correction
Date of birth (DOB) of the nominee
Address: You must submit ID proof of the nominee (Aadhaar or Driving Licence). If you submit Aadhaar, ensure you provide a
masked Aadhaar
Relationship
Email ID (Zerodha doesn't allow Rediffmail email registration due to security concerns and email delivery issues.)
Mobile number
If you need to change the nominee, modify details, or add any new details:
Download, print, and fill out the nominee form
(PDF)
and account modification form
(PDF).
Sample account modification form.
(PDF).
Sign both forms (with a wet signature). Ensure your signature matches the
signature provided while opening your Zerodha account.
eSign
both forms.
Create a ticket
and attach both forms.
Nominee details will be updated within 48 working hours.
You cannot delete a nominee online. To delete or opt out of the nominee, you can follow the offline process:
Offline
If you want to remove your nominee:
Download and fill out the account modification form
(PDF)
and annexure 1B
(PDF).
Sample account modification form for nominee deletion
(PDF).
Sign both forms. Ensure your signature matches the
signature provided while opening your Zerodha account.
Courier the forms to the address below.
Courier address:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Nominee details will be updated within 3 working days after Zerodha receives the forms.
Things to keep in mind
Zerodha charges ₹25 + 18% GST for account modifications.
Ensure the nominee's names match their documents to facilitate easier claims in adverse situations.
If Zerodha deactivates your account due to
inactivity
for 24 consecutive months and you do not fill out Re-KYC within 30 days,
Zerodha sends an email to your nominee.
If your nominee is a minor, provide the guardian's details and ID proof. You cannot add the account holder as the nominee's guardian.
Your nominee applies to your trading and demat account, including mutual fund investments through Coin.
Joint account holders can only add nominees offline.
NRIs can add nominees online if their Aadhaar is linked to their active mobile number.
You cannot add charitable trusts as nominees.
Dormant account holders must
reactivate their account
before modifying or changing a nominee.

title: How to verify the nominee(s) details associated with a Zerodha account?
text:
To verify the nominee(s) details associated with a Zerodha account, follow these steps:
Log in to
console.zerodha.com.
Click on
Account.
Click on
Nominees.
The nominee(s) details can also be verified on the CMR copy. To download the CMR, see
What is a CMR copy and where can I get it?
To learn how to add a nominee online, see
How to add a nominee to the Zerodha account online?

title: What is the inactivity alert?
text:
If you have not traded for 24 consecutive months, the exchange marks your account as dormant and deactivates it. To reactivate your account, you need to verify your KYC (Know Your Customer) details.
If you do not reactivate your account within 30 days of it becoming dormant, Zerodha sends an inactivity alert to the nominee(s) on record. This alert keeps your nominees informed. If you want to unsubscribe from these alerts, you can
create a ticket.
To learn more, see
Introducing online nomination.
Did you know?
The nominee can also
notify or claim shares after the death of a Zerodha account holder.

title: How to add a beneficiary on CDSL to transfer securities?
text:
You must add the receiver's demat account as a beneficiary on CDSL before transferring securities off-market. CDSL requires this step to prevent incorrect transfers, as per their circular
(PDF).
Adding a beneficiary on CDSL
Follow these steps to add a beneficiary:
Visit
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/Home/Login.
Login using Easi or Easiest credentials. If the account isn’t registered, register with
Easi
or
Easiest.
Click on
Miscellanous.
Click on
BO Linking.
Click on BO
Linking Setup.
Enter buyer’s (receiver’s)
BO ID
and PAN.
Click on
Submit.
CDSL will send you an email to verify the beneficiary details. You need to complete verification by following these steps:
Click on the link received in the email.
Enter seller's (transferor's)
PAN
or
Demat Account No.
The
demat account number
is the 16-digit ID.
Click on
BO SELLER BUYER LINK.
Click on
Submit.
Tick on the beneficiary details and click on
Generate OTP.
Enter the OTP and click on
Accept.
Click on
OK.
The beneficiary is added instantly. Once added, you can transfer shares using
Delivery Instruction Slip (DIS),
CDSL Easiest
or
Zerodha's gift feature.
Things to keep in mind
You can check your beneficiary status (whether it's delinked, pending, or verified) by navigating to
Miscellaneous > BO linking > BO linking inquiry
and
delink.
You can also add a beneficiary by filling and sending Annexure A
(PDF)
to
Zerodha's office.
However, you still need to verify the beneficiary through the email link as explained above.

title: Why was an email from CDSL received stating PAN Verification Reversed?
text:
As of June 3rd, 2024, PAN and aadhaar linking is not mandatory (
NSE
,
BSE
). Clients can now trade and invest provided all other requisite conditions are met, including having a valid name, PAN, mobile number, email ID, complete address, income details, and KRA compliance.
As per the regulations
(WEB),
trading and investing will be disabled if the PAN is not linked with Aadhaar by 30th June 2023. CDSL will validate if the PAN from the Income Tax Database (ITD) is linked to Aadhaar and send an email if it is not.
It can take up to 30 working days for the PAN to be updated in IT records. To learn more, see
How to link PAN with Aadhaar?
Clients can ignore the email if the PAN is linked to their Aadhaar.

title: How to upgrade from Myeasi to Myeasiest?
text:
You can upgrade from Myeasi to Myeasiest by following these steps:
Log in to Myeasi by visiting
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/home/login.
Click on
Miscellaneous
and then on
Upgrade BO ID to Easiest.
3. Select
Trusted account (PIN)
as the
Account Type,
agree to the terms and conditions and click
Submit.
4. Enter the
BO ID
of the
trusted account
to which the securities are being transferred and click on
Submit.
5. An upgrade request is sent to Zerodha. Non-individuals must print the prefilled registration form or print it
(PDF)
and courier it to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
6. Zerodha will authenticate it within 24 working hours. Once authenticated, the BO ID will be activated instantly.
Understanding the difference
MyEASI (Electronic Access to Securities Information) allows you to only view your demat account details.
MyEASIEST (Electronic Access to Securities Information and Execution of Secured Transactions) allows you to
submit off-market instructions and execute online transactions.
With Myeasiest, you don't need to submit physical DIS (Delivery Instructions Slip) forms to your Depository Participant (DP) for transactions.

title: Why did CDSL send an email with the transaction details?
text:
CDSL sends you transaction emails because it acts as your securities depository, holding and maintaining your investments on your behalf. You receive these emails as a regulatory requirement to keep you informed about all activities in your demat account. CDSL automatically sends you an email and SMS containing details of debits and credits on the T+1 day when your transactions are settled.
Zerodha also sends you a monthly
holdings statement
with details of all debits and credits in your demat account for the given month.

title: How to reset the password or view the login name for CDSL Easi?
text:
You can reset your
CDSL Easi
password or retrieve your login name through CDSL's online recovery options. Both processes require verification through your registered mobile number and email address. To reset the CDSL Easi password, follow these steps:
Visit the
CDSL forgot password page.
Enter the login name
1
and PAN.
Click on
Submit.
An email with a password reset link will be sent to the registered email ID.
Click on the link in the email.
Enter the OTP sent to the mobile number and the email ID.
Click on
Submit.
Enter the new password.
Click on
Submit.
1
If the login name is unknown, follow these steps:
Visit the
CDSL forgot username page.
Enter
BO ID.
Enter the PAN.
Click on
Submit.
Enter the OTP sent to the mobile number and email ID.
Click on
Submit.

title: How to register for CDSL Easiest using the Zerodha demat account?
text:
You can register for
CDSL Easiest
online if you have an individual demat account.
Follow these steps to register for CDSL Easiest:
Visit
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/Home/EasiestRegistration.
Click on the
Click here
button next to
To Register for Easiest!
Demat details:
Enter the
8-digit DP ID followed by the 8-digit demat client ID or BO ID
and click on
Continue.
Enter the OTP you received on your registered mobile number or email and click on
Continue.
Account details:
In the account details section, enter the preferred username, answer the security question, select the
Trusted Account
checkbox and click on
Continue.
If you have an individual Demat account, your request will be approved by Zerodha within 24 working hours, and you will receive your login password on your registered email ID.
If you are a non-individual Demat account holder,
create ticket
with Zerodha, after which you will receive the required form in the ticket response.
Download the form, take a printout, fill in all the required details, and courier the duly filled and signed form to to Zerodha’s office at the following address:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A, 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru – 560076
Once the form is received and verified, your request will be approved, and you will receive your login password on your registered email ID.
Account types
Trusted account transfer:
Trusted accounts
are those demat accounts where a client wants to transfer their securities through easiest.
Trusted account transfer permits the transfer of securities from one client to another. Account holders (Beneficiary Owners) can transfer the securities to four CDSL demat accounts of their own choice, which have been added as trusted accounts. Transactions through the trusted account option can be set up and have to be approved by an additional PIN, which will be emailed to the BO separately. If the BO opts for the trusted account option, they will receive two separate emails with two separate passwords, one for accessing the easiest login and the second for the PIN for authorising their transactions.
Account of choice transfer:
Account of choice transfer permits the transfer of securities from one account holder (BO) account to another demat account. Transactions are authenticated through a Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) (e-token), which the BO has to obtain from any of the Registered Authority (RA).
The BO will have to submit the easiest registration form signed by all the holders (not applicable for sole holders) and the other Registering Authority (RA) form for mapping the digital signature (e-token) to the DP for authentication.
Transfers can be done across the depositories, i.e, CDSL to CDSL and
CDSL to NSDL.
Activation
After you complete registration, Zerodha will authenticate your details, and CDSL will activate Easiest for your BO ID. The activation process takes up to 48 working hours. You need to
add a beneficiary on CDSL to transfer securities.
Once  the beneficiary is added, you can
transfer shares from your Zerodha demat account to other CDSL demat accounts using CDSL Easiest.

title: Why did I receive an email from CDSL stating UCC is delinked from my demat account?
text:
You received this email because another broker has changed your client ID status from Active to Inactive or Closed. This change automatically delinks your Unique Client Code (UCC) from your Zerodha demat account. You can ignore this email as it does not affect your trading activity with Zerodha.
Understanding UCC mapping
SEBI mandates
that brokers map your Unique Client Code (UCC) with your
demat account number (BO ID)
to establish systematic checks. This requirement ensures that shares you sell are debited from your account and shares you purchase are credited to your account.
The system maps your UCC with:
Your Zerodha demat account number
Zerodha's trading member ID with the exchange
Zerodha's clearing member ID with the clearing corporation
Account segments you have enabled (NSE, BSE, NFO, CDS, etc.)
When you open a Zerodha trading and demat account, this mapping occurs automatically on the backend systems of exchanges (NSE and BSE), depositories (CDSL), and clearing corporations.
Multiple broker accounts
If you have trading accounts with multiple brokers, your Zerodha demat account gets mapped to all your unique client codes. For example, if you have 5 trading accounts with 5 different stockbrokers (including Zerodha), your Zerodha demat account will be mapped to 5 different Unique Client Codes.
When delinking occurs
The mapping between your UCC and demat account changes when any broker modifies your UCC status. When another broker changes your client ID status from Active to Inactive or Closed, the system automatically delinks that UCC from your Zerodha demat account.
CDSL sends you an email notification whenever this delinking occurs. In the email, 'V' stands for Verified and 'D' stands for Delinked.
These changes do not affect your trading activity with Zerodha. CDSL informs you about system changes the day after they occur, which is why you receive these notification emails.

title: How to transfer shares from Zerodha to NSDL demat account via CDSL Easiest?
text:
You can transfer shares from your Zerodha demat account to an NSDL demat account using CDSL Easiest. You need to
register for CDSL Easiest
as a Trusted account,
add a beneficiary,
purchase an e-token, and complete the verification process. Once CDSL maps your e-token, you can transfer shares online.
Purchase and verify the E-token
Purchase an e-token from authorised e-token vendors. The e-token costs approximately ₹2500 plus GST and remains valid for up to two years. The cost may vary depending on the vendor.
Fill the Request of Authorisation Form (RA form)
(PDF)
and print screenshots of your e-token certificate.
To find your e-token certificate on Internet Explorer
Open
Internet Explorer.
Click on
Tools
and then
Internet Options.
Click on
Content
and then
Certificates.
Take screenshots of these tabs:
General tab
Details tab: Serial number
Details tab: Subject alternative name
Details tab: Subject
For Windows users: Search for Internet options in the search bar > Content > click on Certificates, double-click on your e-token details, and share screenshots of all three tabs (General, Details, Certificate path).
For e-mudhra token users: Install the Embridge application on Windows OS to
verify your e-token on e-mudhra.
Courier these documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
You will receive a confirmation email once Zerodha receives your forms and e-token certificates via courier.
Change mode of operation
After receiving the confirmation email, change your mode of operation from Trusted to Account of Choice:
Log in to CDSL Easiest by visiting
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/home/login.
Click on
Miscellaneous
and then on
Modify Mode of Operation.
Click on
Submit.
CDSL will map your e-token within 20 working days. Once CDSL maps your e-token, you can transfer shares online.
Create a ticket
with the RA form, a screenshot of the e-token certificate, and a screenshot of the e-token registration.
Transfer shares online
Log in to CDSL Easiest, click on
Transaction
and then on
Setup.
Select
Bulk Setup.
Select
Interdepository,
enter the execution date, enter the Target ID (BOID) you are transferring the shares to, select the Entity identifier as NA, and select type as NA.
Select the ISIN. Click on Account ISINs to see all the ISINs in your holdings. Select the ISIN, enter the quantity, and select the reason for trade from the list. Refer to the CDSL reason code guide
(PDF)
to select the correct reason code as per CDSL requirements to avoid rejection. You do not need to pay any amount if the transfer is a gift, own account transfer, or transfer to family members. You can keep the consideration amount as "0"
Review the ISIN details and click on
Submit
to set up and then on
Verify.
You do not need to fill in
the Exchange ID, Counter Party Exchange ID, Market Type, Counter Party Market Type, Settlement ID,
and
Counter Settlement ID
for off-market transactions. Click on Submit and enter your e-token password to authenticate the transaction.
Your depository participant (DP) will receive the request for confirmation. Once your DP confirms, your request will be approved, and the transfer will be completed.
Things to keep in mind
You can follow the
offline process
if you need to transfer shares faster than the CDSL Easiest method.
Transfer charges of ₹25 per security, per transaction, plus 18% GST apply. For example, if you transfer 10 shares of HDFC, 5 shares of Reliance and 2 shares of Infosys in one transaction, the charges would be ₹75 plus 18% GST. If you transfer 10 shares of HDFC in two transactions, the charges would be ₹50 plus 18% GST. You must maintain the required balance for the charges to be debited.
Your share transfer request will be rejected if your trading account has a zero or negative balance, or if your account is dormant. If you add funds to clear a negative balance, the funds will be reflected by the end of the day (EOD), and you can initiate the transfer process on the following day. If your account is dormant, you must complete
re-KYC
to reactivate it before proceeding with the share transfer.
To avoid rejection of your share transfer, do not sell shares you are transferring before the approval of the easiest transaction. In rare cases, the sale transaction may not be rejected and can go to auction. In these cases, you are responsible for auction charges.
You cannot transfer shares and mutual funds under lock-in, pledge or
frozen status
using this flow.
You cannot transfer inactive ISINs.
Transfer requests will not be processed if your
account is frozen.
If the reason for transfer is due to a sale (For Off-Market Sale/Purchase) and you are receiving money, enter the sale amount under Consideration Amount. Select the Payment mode of receiving the money and enter the bank details of the buyer. You must also pay stamp duty for that sale to CDSL
(PDF)
via your virtual account number
(PDF)
before making the transfer. Visit
cdslindia.com/stampduty/cal_stampduty.aspx
to learn how much stamp duty you must pay.

title: How to add a trusted account on CDSL Easiest?
text:
You can add a trusted account on
CDSL Easiest
to transfer stocks from your demat account. You need to add accounts as trusted before you can transfer securities to them through CDSL Easiest.
Adding a trusted account
Follow these steps to add a trusted account:
Log in to
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/home/login.
Click on
Miscellaneous
and then
Edit Trusted Account.
3. Click on
Add Trusted Account
and enter the 16-digit CDSL BO ID where you want to transfer stocks. If you are adding a Zerodha account as trusted, you can
find the BO ID on Console.
Securities transferred to an incorrect BO ID cannot be reversed, similar to a fund transfer to the wrong bank account.
Your Depository Participant (DP) will approve your trusted account within 24 working hours, after which you can transfer stocks to that account.
Things to keep in mind
You can only add CDSL demat accounts as trusted accounts using this process
You can map up to 4 trusted accounts at any time
If you need to add a new account when you already have 4 trusted accounts, you must delete an existing mapped account first
Depository Participant (DP) approves deletion requests within 24 working hours
You can also
transfer shares from Zerodha to NSDL demat accounts via CDSL easiest.
However, you cannot add an NSDL BO ID as a trusted account, and shares can only be transferred by selecting the account of choice transfer option.
If someone wants to transfer shares from another CDSL broker to your Zerodha account, they need to log in to
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/home/login
with their existing broker details and add your Zerodha account as their trusted account.
For joint and non-individual accounts
If you have a joint or non-individual account, you need to courier the prefilled Trusted Account Form with both transferor and transferee signatures to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,

Bengaluru, 560076

title: Can unlisted securities be bought using the Zerodha demat account?
text:
No, you cannot buy unlisted securities using your Zerodha demat account. However, you can hold unlisted securities electronically in your Zerodha demat account if the securities are available in the depository.
You can transfer unlisted securities to and from your Zerodha demat account through
online
and
offline
method.
Lock-in period for unlisted shares
If you hold unlisted shares of a company that goes public, regulations require you to wait 6 months before selling these shares. This mandatory lock-in period begins from the company's listing date.
Things to keep in mind
Your unlisted securities will not appear on Kite. You can view them on Console by visiting
console.zerodha.com/portfolio/holdings.
You should exercise due diligence when exploring unlisted securities investment opportunities, as the market for these securities can be illiquid.

title: What is CDSL Easi and how to register for it?
text:
CDSL Easi is a portal by Central Depository Services (CDSL) where you can view, track, and download your demat holdings. You can register for this service online using your demat account details.
Registration process
Follow these steps to register for CDSL Easi:
Visit
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/Home/EasiRegistration.
Under
BO ID,
enter the
16 digit demat ID.
Under
Password,
enter PAN along with the date and month of birth and click on
Continue.
For example, if the PAN number is ALWPG5809L and the DOB is 25-03-1993, enter ALWPG5809L2503.
Enter the OTP sent to the registered email ID or mobile number and click on
Continue.
Enter a username, password and click on
Continue.
If you hold multiple demat accounts, you can enter the demat IDs of your other accounts to group them together. Grouping allows you to access multiple demat accounts using a single user ID. Click on
Continue
to complete your registration successfully.
Once registered, you can also
download your statement of holdings (SOH) and statement of transactions (SOT) using CDSL Easi.
You can
upgrade from Myeasi to Myeasiest from the CDSL website.

title: What is CDSL Easiest?
text:
CDSL Easiest (Electronic Access to Securities Information and Execution of Secured Transactions) is an internet-based facility that allows you to submit various transaction instructions directly from your demat account. CDSL offers this service to enable secure online securities transactions.
CDSL Easiest can be used to
transfer securities from one demat to another online.
You can
register for CDSL Easiest using your Zerodha account.
However, if you have already registered in Myeasi, you can
upgrade from Myeasi to Myeasiest.
What you can do with Easiest
Through CDSL Easiest, you can submit the following types of transaction instructions:
Off-market
On-market
Inter-depository
Early pay-in debit instructions
When you subscribe to Easiest, you automatically receive all the facilities and benefits of Electronic Access to Securities Information (Easi), which allows you to view your demat account details and transaction history.

title: How to download transaction cum holding (SOT and SOH) statement?
text:
You can download your transaction cum holding statement from CDSL Easi regardless of your account type (individual, joint, NRI, or non-individual). You need to
register for CDSL Easi
before downloading statements.
Downloading the statement
Login to
web.cdslindia.com/myeasitoken/home/login.
Click on
Transaction
and then
Transaction cum Holding Statement.
Select
History
Report,
enter the date range and click on
Submit.
Click on
Download
to download the statement or click on
Email
to send the statement to the registered email ID.
Monthly statements from Zerodha
Zerodha automatically sends monthly holding statements to your registered email address at the end of every month. These statements include all debit and credit transactions for your equity and mutual fund holdings during the month.

title: Is it legal to continue carrying out transactions in a deceased person’s Zerodha account?
text:
No, it is not legal to transact in a deceased person's Zerodha account. Indian law prohibits transactions in accounts belonging to deceased individuals. If you are a nominee or legal heir, you can
claim the funds and securities by transferring them to your own demat and bank accounts after submitting the required documents.
Things to keep in mind
Transacting in a deceased person's account constitutes impersonation and may result in penalties or criminal charges under Indian law. This restriction applies to market transactions, off-market transactions through gifting and transactions using DIS slips.
If a transaction occurs in the deceased's account after the date of death mentioned in the death certificate, the transmission process will be delayed due to increased scrutiny.

title: How to claim shares from an NSDL account without a nominee if holdings exceed ₹15 lakhs?
text:
If the deceased’s account has holdings and funds worth more than ₹15 lakhs, the following documents must be submitted by the applicant to notify and claim the shares:
Annexure 1
(PDF)
Transmission Form 7.1
(PDF)
Death certificate of the deceased holder. Original/notarised/attested by a gazetted officer
Self-attested copy of the PAN of the successor/legal heir/applicant/deponent/claimant
A personalised cancelled cheque or a bank statement/passbook not older than 3 months of successor(s)/Legal heirs(s). (Only applicable if the deceased bank account has a positive balance)
CMR hard copy of the successor/legal heir/applicant/deponent/claimant with the DP seal and signature. If the account is with Zerodha, then a CMR is not required.
Affidavit
(PDF)
to be submitted by the successor/legal heir/applicant/deponent/claimant on a
separate
stamp paper valued at ₹100. The successor/legal heir/applicant/deponent/claimant is the 1st party, and Zerodha Broking LTD would be the 2nd party.
Anyone of the following:
Succession certificate.
Letter of administration.
The probate of the will.
Will and Bond of Indemnity
(PDF)
from all the applicants or non-applicants on a single stamp paper.
Court decree.
Legal heir certificate issued by the court, Bond of Indemnity
(PDF)
from all the applicants and NOC
(PDF)
from all the non-applicants, both on a single stamp paper. In the BOI, the successor/legal heir/applicant/deponent/claimant is the 1st party, and Zerodha Broking LTD would be the 2nd party. In the NOC, the non-applicants are the 1st party, and Zerodha Broking LTD would be the 2nd party.
Courier the documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Did you know?
Once Zerodha's legal team confirms that the received documents are valid, the transmission process will be completed within 7 days.
Lock-in shares will only be moved to the successor NSDL demat accounts.
Zerodha fetches the KYC of the clients as per SEBI's requirements.
If available on the government portal, a photocopy or soft copy of the digital death certificate will be considered.
A photocopy of the death certificate can be considered if it is available on the government website. Clients can share the government link where the death certificate can be viewed or downloaded by creating a ticket.
The credit fund balance of the deceased will be transferred to the successor or nominee’s bank account mentioned in the CMR.
If the deceased has a debit balance, the successor/nominee must provide a cheque in the name of Zerodha Broking Ltd.
The deceased name should match CMR and death certificate. If not, submit a name mismatch notarised affidavit.
The successor/nominee's name should match the CMR. If not, submit the name mismatch notarised affidavit.
If the nominee/successor/legal heir is a minor, provide a guardian certificate to consider the guardian's signature in the form. A guardian certificate is not required if a minor has an account with Zerodha.

title: How can a nominee claim shares from an NSDL demat account after the death of the account holder?
text:
The nominee must submit the following documents for a death claim:
Annexure 1
(PDF)
Transmission Form 7.1
(PDF)
Death certificate of the deceased holder original/ notarized/attested by a gazetted officer
PAN or any other valid ID proof of the deceased attested by the notifier
Self-attested PAN of the nominee/s
A personalised cancelled cheque or a bank statement/passbook not older than 3 months of Nominee/s(If deceased a/c have positive ledger balance once)
CMR hard copy of the nominee(s) with DP seal and signature. If the nominee has an account with Zerodha, then a CMR is not required
Courier the documents to:
Zerodha Customer Support Centre
192A 4th Floor,
Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road,
JP Nagar 4th Phase,
Bengaluru, 560076
Things to keep in mind
Once Zerodha receives the valid documents, the transmission process will be completed within 7 days. There are no charges for the transmission process.
Lock-in shares will only be moved to the successor or the nominee’s NSDL demat accounts.
A photocopy of the death certificate can be considered if it is available on the government website. Clients can
create a ticket
to share the link where the death certificate can be viewed or downloaded.
The credit fund balance of the deceased will be transferred to the successor or nominee’s bank account mentioned in the CMR.
If the deceased has a debit balance, the successor/nominee must provide a cheque in the name of Zerodha Broking Ltd.
The deceased name should match the CMR and death certificate. If not, submit a name mismatch notarised affidavit.
The nominee's name should match the CMR. If not, submit the Name mismatch notarised affidavit.
If the nominee/successor/legal heir is a minor, provide a guardian certificate to consider the guardian's signature in the form. A guardian certificate is not required if a minor has an account with Zerodha.

title: How can shares be claimed from an NSDL account without a nominee if holdings are under ₹15 lakhs?
text:
To notify and claim the shares if a nominee was not appointed and the funds and holdings are worth less than ₹15 lakhs, submit the following documents:

Once Zerodha receives the valid documents, the transmission process will be completed within 7 days.

If available on the government portal, a photocopy or soft copy of the digital death certificate will be considered.
A photocopy of the death certificate can be considered if it is available on the government website. Clients can share the government link where the death certificate can be viewed or downloaded by creating a ticket.
Zerodha fetches the KYC of the clients as per SEBI's requirements.
The credit fund balance of the deceased will be transferred to the successor or nominee’s bank account mentioned in the CMR.
If the deceased has a debit balance, the successor/nominee must provide a cheque in the name of Zerodha Broking Ltd.
